| | Dear Friend of Liberty, How do you feel about the recent election results? I’m asking because I’ve heard from many conservatives who are dismayed about the recent elections, having hoped for a wave election that could win both houses of Congress. Now, with a divided legislature, we most likely face two years of gridlock. But that doesn’t paint the entire picture. Constitutional conservatives like you and I can win victories against the Biden administration right now. **The place we can win right now is in the courts.** That’s where, in the last year, our attorneys are winning battles against the Biden administration's attempts to redefine the definition of a firearm, to force pronoun usage in public schools, and to unlawfully tell private landowners what they can and cannot do with their land. The court system is the ultimate safeguard of our constitutional rights. The court system is designed to ensure that the government does not overstep the boundaries of its powers and is held accountable for its actions. **It is essential that we continue to fight to uphold the Constitution and protect our rights, especially since we face unprecedented times as we head into 2023.** Our attorneys are leading the fight to restore and protect your rights against an insatiable, overreaching government. Our attorneys litigate crucial cases at no legal cost to our clients to defend their freedoms. And when we win, you win. If you are frustrated with the state of government in DC, I’d like to ask for your support of our attorneys in courthouses across the western United States. That’s where they fight battles to defend your right to own a gun, own land, and speak freely. [**Would you help fund these attorneys, who work countless hours on behalf of our brave clients who are standing up against the Biden administration?**](http://links.reclamingamericanfreedom.com/u/click?_t=5ec072595b1347f09b4bac0de82ea87d&_m=151133329db643c7b9f0ea6b9c6f2279&_e=FS-9kABVlEIFk-jCk7aCZARn6Zc0j1nBPVcZqXBIlhA8Y8Pb2BK7WaAVLfetfa2fqWrFoBYMo3d7hYMwmtwK17hGbw1xq2GaQin9QeuaTZmTsm9WSrh15duSXQ_6eLYZHiP3nGYkKOUtuD-d8FaT8ssl-7cRkNYg4i2SF9YB8GQb34l6fgsj_IKhNGRm7aqgzUNPihGt-yEAUKHzmvwJ_fJR1KzGEARanhtdgilhuvPZnDocV_EHGvTWULJr0Br9pMXxeAA8fPwuPg8Cpiyx3KYxdvfAT4b956Ab8UyerGdYSavecTIdsh32hCEw3VoMacC4tz3Mr-2_QJyCE52pJw%3D%3D) **Your gift of $25, $50, or even $250 today** can fund an attorney who can WIN battles to defend your Constitutional rights. [**Will you contribute to these efforts by making a gift today?**](http://links.reclamingamericanfreedom.com/u/click?_t=5ec072595b1347f09b4bac0de82ea87d&_m=151133329db643c7b9f0ea6b9c6f2279&_e=FS-9kABVlEIFk-jCk7aCZARn6Zc0j1nBPVcZqXBIlhA8Y8Pb2BK7WaAVLfetfa2fqWrFoBYMo3d7hYMwmtwK17hGbw1xq2GaQin9QeuaTZmTsm9WSrh15duSXQ_6eLYZHiP3nGYkKOUtuD-d8FaT8ssl-7cRkNYg4i2SF9YB8GQb34l6fgsj_IKhNGRm7aqgOdmw918wQpy54VR-55Rw5VOtQPSWpwfhj7ndngPfXl2Z37_WukvspyU3huBPpSvyvpOkzB_hkLvewXNh-TNIXmvEpdNoWY4Ekoehq1pdVNr2o7UVTHqTfJ-XW4s6X4YFnG1vUD5NM2KrVmxHljAbMQ%3D%3D) Here’s the secure link where you can donate to protect your constitutional rights: **[https://mslegal.org/donate/](http://links.reclamingamericanfreedom.com/u/click?_t=5ec072595b1347f09b4bac0de82ea87d&_m=151133329db643c7b9f0ea6b9c6f2279&_e=FS-9kABVlEIFk-jCk7aCZARn6Zc0j1nBPVcZqXBIlhA8Y8Pb2BK7WaAVLfetfa2fqWrFoBYMo3d7hYMwmtwK17hGbw1xq2GaQin9QeuaTZmTsm9WSrh15duSXQ_6eLYZHiP3nGYkKOUtuD-d8FaT8ssl-7cRkNYg4i2SF9YB8GQb34l6fgsj_IKhNGRm7aqg7o648fwy75fy1gndat--Nbvw4n_WGtwOO1P0NK99cvj3_c6H3SBByCnsJACLGGv8cOywyIDBmghBY63cayt_2dhjryZXDcmfbtV1Qt0QHGAPns15LReszPUBiBVnjgIVGEYsfvoC7BW9uUjzrtH1fg%3D%3D)** Your gift will secure a victory to tell the government not to trample on our individual rights, and will show support to your fellow brave Americans who are fighting every day to ensure your liberties are kept intact. For Liberty! Cristen Wohlgemuth President & CEO Mountain States Legal Foundation

|

| **General Assembly begins 2023 legislative session Jan. 4**
The 102nd Missouri General Assembly gets underway Jan. 4 with opportunities for lawmakers to use an unprecedented $6 billion budget surplus to make transformative investments in the state tempered by a pending crash in revenue collections as a massive election-season income tax cut majority Republicans enacted in September takes effect.

As of Jan. 1, the individual income tax rate paid by most Missourians will drop sharply from 5.3 percent to 4.8 percent. Additional cuts will kick in over the coming years to eventually drop the income tax rate 4.5 percent. The cuts are expected to cost the state about $2 billion a year in lost revenue once fully implemented.

Through the first five months of FY 2023, net state general revenue collections had increased a whopping 14.6 percent compared to the previous year. With the new tax cuts, however, state budget officials predict growth of just 1.3 percent for the year by the time FY 2023 ends on June 30. For FY 2024, budget officials expected net general revenue growth of a mere 0.7 percent.

While the current budget surplus the state acquired from an influx of federal pandemic relief funds and inflation-driven growth in sales and income tax collections is temporary, the tax cuts are permanent. If in coming years state government has insufficient revenue to pay for basic operations, lawmakers cannot reverse the cuts on their own and would need voter approval for any significant increase.

Although Democrats picked up three seats in the House of Representatives during the November general election, Republicans will again have veto-proof supermajorities in both chambers, outnumbering Democrats 111-52 in the House and 24-10 in the Senate, where the partisan breakdown remains unchanged from last year.

Republicans are expected to use their supermajorities to take another run at limiting Missourians’ power to enact legislation via the initiative petition process, with dozens of proposals on the subject already filed. The initiative has been used several times in recent years to circumvent the legislature on high-profile issues such as Medicaid expansion, marijuana legalization and legislative ethics reform, among others Republican lawmakers had opposed.

Although a near total ban on abortion, including those involving cases of rape or incest, took effect in Missouri this summer following the conservative U.S. Supreme Court’s elimination of abortion as a constitutional right, Republicans are expected to attempt to restrict Missouri women from getting abortions in states where it remains legal. Other Republican priorities include prohibiting public schools from teaching about the past and present impacts of racism in society, banning libraries from carrying books Republicans don’t like and limiting LBGT rights.

Democrats plan to push for more robust state support for public education, including higher minimum teacher salaries to address a continuing shortage of qualified teachers in many school districts. They also will advocate for expanded access to post-partum care for women and infants, as well as protection and expansion of voting rights. In addition, strengthening gun safety laws will be a top Democratic priority to reverse a spike in violent crime that has resulted from Republicans weakening those laws in recent years.

The 2023 legislative session runs through May 12. Lawmakers face a May 5 constitutional deadline for passing the FY 2024 state operating budget.
---
|
---
| **Court again blocks attempts to defund Planned Parenthood** The latest attempt by Republican lawmakers to prohibit Planned Parenthood from receiving Medicaid reimbursements for providing non-abortion health care services to women is unenforceable, a Cole County judge ruled Dec. 28. The decision tracks a 2020 Missouri Supreme Court decision striking down similar restrictions lawmakers attempted to impose in the state budget.

For several years majority Republicans in the General Assembly have unsuccessfully attempted to prohibit Planned Parenthood from participating Missouri’s Medicaid program, known as MO HealthNet, by crafting restrictive appropriations language purporting to bar the organization from receiving Medicaid funds. In its 2020 ruling, the Supreme Court said that under the Missouri Constitution appropriation bills must be limited to appropriating funds and cannot establish policy, which the restrictions on Planned Parenthood attempt to do.

Judge Jon Beetem, a Republican, issued his ruling in a brief order and said funds from the Medicaid appropriation are available to all service providers. Beetem’s order also bars the state from refusing to reimburse Planned Parenthood for providing eligible services in the future.

“Language purporting to deny Plaintiffs access to funds that are otherwise available to other MO HealthNet providers is ineffective and/or unconstitutional,” Beetem wrote. “Defendants are enjoined from denying any claim of reimbursement for MO HealthNet services made by Plaintiffs solely due to lack of an appropriation.”

The state could appeal Beetem’s ruling but likely would not prevail given the Supreme Court’s established precedent on the issue. But the latest decision probably won’t thwart legislative Republicans from inserting the unconstitutional and unenforceable restrictions in the next state budget. The case is _Planned Parenthood of the St. Louis Region, et al., v. Robert Knodell, et al._

|

[_**SB 146**_](https://robertsforstl.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6e3f7ee3b541518ea63c7e773&id=5c31be6517&e=a088917f89) \- Creates provisions relating to compensation for wrongful convictions

[_**SB 147**_](https://robertsforstl.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6e3f7ee3b541518ea63c7e773&id=2d83441267&e=a088917f89) \- Modifies provisions relating to parole eligibility

[_**SB 215**_](https://robertsforstl.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6e3f7ee3b541518ea63c7e773&id=66c0c3e5ee&e=a088917f89) \- Establishes provisions relating to alternative dispute resolution

[**_SB 216_**](https://robertsforstl.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6e3f7ee3b541518ea63c7e773&id=d1834545e0&e=a088917f89) \- Designates "James W Brooks Memorial Highway" in St. Louis City and St. Louis County

| **HELP FOR SMALL BUSINESS AND NON- PROFITS **
The U.S. Small Business Administration approved Missouri’s request for assistance through the federal agency’s disaster loan program. This move makes low-interest disaster loans available to small businesses and private non-profits severely impacted by the Coronavirus (COVID-19).

SBA’s Economic Injury Disaster Loans offer up to $2 million in assistance for each affected small business or non-profit. These loans can provide vital economic support to small businesses to help overcome the temporary loss of revenue they are experiencing.

Applicants will be able to apply online, receive additional disaster assistance information and [download applications](https://robertsforstl.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6e3f7ee3b541518ea63c7e773&id=358f888fce&e=a088917f89). Applicants may also call SBA’s Customer Service Center at (800) 659-2955 or email [disastercustomerservice@sba.gov](mailto:disastercustomerservice@sba.gov) for more information on SBA disaster assistance.

If you or someone you know needs help, please visit [Safe Connections](https://robertsforstl.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6e3f7ee3b541518ea63c7e773&id=cfb62ba9d4&e=a088917f89) or call _314-531-2003_.

| **RX OUTREACH EXPANDS ELIGIBILITY **
Below is a memo from a trusted non-profit:

As a non-profit, mail-order pharmacy, Rx Outreach is uniquely positioned to help stop the spread of COVID-19 for people who are quarantined and those who may be afraid to travel outside of their homes to the clinic or pharmacy.

Rx Outreach lifted its income guidelines during this crisis so that **anyone, regardless of income, can participate in their program.**

Rx Outreach medication prices are generally 70-80% below retail and are transparent. Shipping is free.

---
|
---
| **LOW-COST INSULIN** Before COVID-19 insulin users were having a difficult time affording medication due to the tripling of the cost over the last decade. In the wake of COVID-19, it has become even more difficult for those who rely on insulin to afford the inflated cost. Eli Lilly and Company has introduced a program to purchase Basaglar insulin at significant cost savings. Please call Lilly Diabetes Solution Center at 833-808-1234 between 9 am and 9 pm, M-F to see if you qualify for these savings. More information can be found at the [Eli Lilly and Co. Website](https://robertsforstl.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6e3f7ee3b541518ea63c7e773&id=bbf891b29a&e=a088917f89).

clean_body: The 102nd Missouri General Assembly gets underway Jan. 4 with opportunities for lawmakers to use an unprecedented $6 billion budget surplus | | | | | | | Capitol Report December 30, 2022 | | | | Contact Senator Roberts 201 W. Capitol Ave., Rm 329 Jefferson City, MO 65101 (573) 751-4415 Steve.Roberts@senate.mo.gov | | | | | | | | | | | | General Assembly begins 2023 legislative session Jan. 4 The 102nd Missouri General Assembly gets underway Jan. 4 with opportunities for lawmakers to use an unprecedented $6 billion budget surplus to make transformative investments in the state tempered by a pending crash in revenue collections as a massive election-season income tax cut majority Republicans enacted in September takes effect. As of Jan. 1, the individual income tax rate paid by most Missourians will drop sharply from 5.3 percent to 4.8 percent. Additional cuts will kick in over the coming years to eventually drop the income tax rate 4.5 percent. The cuts are expected to cost the state about $2 billion a year in lost revenue once fully implemented. Through the first five months of FY 2023, net state general revenue collections had increased a whopping 14.6 percent compared to the previous year. With the new tax cuts, however, state budget officials predict growth of just 1.3 percent for the year by the time FY 2023 ends on June 30. For FY 2024, budget officials expected net general revenue growth of a mere 0.7 percent. While the current budget surplus the state acquired from an influx of federal pandemic relief funds and inflation-driven growth in sales and income tax collections is temporary, the tax cuts are permanent. If in coming years state government has insufficient revenue to pay for basic operations, lawmakers cannot reverse the cuts on their own and would need voter approval for any significant increase. Although Democrats picked up three seats in the House of Representatives during the November general election, Republicans will again have veto-proof supermajorities in both chambers, outnumbering Democrats 111-52 in the House and 24-10 in the Senate, where the partisan breakdown remains unchanged from last year. Republicans are expected to use their supermajorities to take another run at limiting Missourians’ power to enact legislation via the initiative petition process, with dozens of proposals on the subject already filed. The initiative has been used several times in recent years to circumvent the legislature on high-profile issues such as Medicaid expansion, marijuana legalization and legislative ethics reform, among others Republican lawmakers had opposed. Although a near total ban on abortion, including those involving cases of rape or incest, took effect in Missouri this summer following the conservative U.S. Supreme Court’s elimination of abortion as a constitutional right, Republicans are expected to attempt to restrict Missouri women from getting abortions in states where it remains legal. Other Republican priorities include prohibiting public schools from teaching about the past and present impacts of racism in society, banning libraries from carrying books Republicans don’t like and limiting LBGT rights. Democrats plan to push for more robust state support for public education, including higher minimum teacher salaries to address a continuing shortage of qualified teachers in many school districts. They also will advocate for expanded access to post-partum care for women and infants, as well as protection and expansion of voting rights. In addition, strengthening gun safety laws will be a top Democratic priority to reverse a spike in violent crime that has resulted from Republicans weakening those laws in recent years. The 2023 legislative session runs through May 12. Lawmakers face a May 5 constitutional deadline for passing the FY 2024 state operating budget. | | Court again blocks attempts to defund Planned Parenthood The latest attempt by Republican lawmakers to prohibit Planned Parenthood from receiving Medicaid reimbursements for providing non-abortion health care services to women is unenforceable, a Cole County judge ruled Dec. 28. The decision tracks a 2020 Missouri Supreme Court decision striking down similar restrictions lawmakers attempted to impose in the state budget. For several years majority Republicans in the General Assembly have unsuccessfully attempted to prohibit Planned Parenthood from participating Missouri’s Medicaid program, known as MO HealthNet, by crafting restrictive appropriations language purporting to bar the organization from receiving Medicaid funds. In its 2020 ruling, the Supreme Court said that under the Missouri Constitution appropriation bills must be limited to appropriating funds and cannot establish policy, which the restrictions on Planned Parenthood attempt to do. Judge Jon Beetem, a Republican, issued his ruling in a brief order and said funds from the Medicaid appropriation are available to all service providers. Beetem’s order also bars the state from refusing to reimburse Planned Parenthood for providing eligible services in the future. “Language purporting to deny Plaintiffs access to funds that are otherwise available to other MO HealthNet providers is ineffective and/or unconstitutional,” Beetem wrote. “Defendants are enjoined from denying any claim of reimbursement for MO HealthNet services made by Plaintiffs solely due to lack of an appropriation.” The state could appeal Beetem’s ruling but likely would not prevail given the Supreme Court’s established precedent on the issue. But the latest decision probably won’t thwart legislative Republicans from inserting the unconstitutional and unenforceable restrictions in the next state budget. The case is Planned Parenthood of the St. Louis Region, et al., v. Robert Knodell, et al. | | 2023 SENATE BILLS \- Creates new provisions relating to financial institutions \- Enacts new law relating to wage range inquiries by employees and prospective employees \- Creates certificates of exemplary conduct and good moral character issued by circuit courts \- Creates provisions relating to compensation for wrongful convictions \- Modifies provisions relating to parole eligibility \- Establishes provisions relating to alternative dispute resolution \- Designates "James W Brooks Memorial Highway" in St. Louis City and St. Louis County \- Creates the offense of unlawful possession by a minor \- Modifies the practice of dentistry to include the prescription and administration of vaccines \- Authorizes counties to freeze the real property assessed values of certain senior citizens | | "Signed By Governor" | "Truly Agreed Bills" | | | | | HELP FOR SMALL BUSINESS AND NON- PROFITS The U.S. Small Business Administration approved Missouri’s request for assistance through the federal agency’s disaster loan program. This move makes low-interest disaster loans available to small businesses and private non-profits severely impacted by the Coronavirus (COVID-19). SBA’s Economic Injury Disaster Loans offer up to $2 million in assistance for each affected small business or non-profit. These loans can provide vital economic support to small businesses to help overcome the temporary loss of revenue they are experiencing. Applicants will be able to apply online, receive additional disaster assistance information and . Applicants may also call SBA’s Customer Service Center at (800) 659-2955 or email mailto:disastercustomerservice@sba.gov for more information on SBA disaster assistance. | | SAFE SHELTERING RESOURCES Sheltering in place can be difficult for anyone, and some of our neighbors might not be safe due to violence at home. If you or someone you know needs help, please visit or call 314-531-2003. You can also contact at 314-993-2777. | | COMMUNITY RESOURCE LIST The has a COVID-19 Community Economic Relief Fund. If you need help paying bills, rent, or food call 866-211-9966. : : | | | | | | MEDICAL RESOURCES The Department of Health COVID-19 hotline is open from 8 a.m. - 10 p.m. Call 314-657-1499 with questions or concerns. The Missouri Department of Health and Senior Services hotline can be reached at tel:%20\(877\%20435-8411). | | | | | | RX OUTREACH EXPANDS ELIGIBILITY Below is a memo from a trusted non-profit: As a non-profit, mail-order pharmacy, Rx Outreach is uniquely positioned to help stop the spread of COVID-19 for people who are quarantined and those who may be afraid to travel outside of their homes to the clinic or pharmacy. Rx Outreach lifted its income guidelines during this crisis so that anyone, regardless of income, can participate in their program. Rx Outreach medication prices are generally 70-80% below retail and are transparent. Shipping is free. | | LOW-COST INSULIN Before COVID-19 insulin users were having a difficult time affording medication due to the tripling of the cost over the last decade. In the wake of COVID-19, it has become even more difficult for those who rely on insulin to afford the inflated cost. Eli Lilly and Company has introduced a program to purchase Basaglar insulin at significant cost savings. Please call Lilly Diabetes Solution Center at 833-808-1234 between 9 am and 9 pm, M-F to see if you qualify for these savings. name: Cliff Schecter, Blue Amp Action
subject: Imagine not having enough diapers to keep your baby clean, dry and healthy? That's the REALITY for 1 in 3 American families.
body:
Make a baby smile!
| | | | |
---
| | |
---
| | | _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/Td4791bf7-93ca-4772-821c-b9bf77bbe103/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._
---
|
---
| |
---
| | | | | Peter, | _**Imagine not having enough diapers to keep your baby clean, dry and healthy? That's the REALITY for 1 in 3 American families.**_ [**_Please join us in donating $5, $10, $25 or whatever you can afford to Sweet Cheeks Diaper Bank! As a reminder, this is a charitable donation, so any gift you give before 12am on New Year's Eve will count as a writeoff on your taxes for 2022._**](https://default.salsalabs.org/Td4be8e62-0379-4f33-b097-89074b1bed27/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) _Diaper need is the lack of sufficient diapers to keep a baby clean, dry and healthy. **Most immediately, diaper need leads to diaper rash, urinary tract infections and other serious health complications for babies. **_ _Equally important, when parents run out of diapers, they can't take their babies to daycare, leaving them unable to get to work or school -- all the things that provide a firm economic base for their family and hope for their future._ [__](https://default.salsalabs.org/T9d6d0fcc-3923-49d8-9a5c-d1ec6817337c/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) _**At an average rate of six diapers per day per child, diapers cost families nearly $100 per month -- a cost not covered by government assistance programs.**_
---

|
---
| | | | | | [**DONATE NOW**](https://default.salsalabs.org/Td6b2fb42-0fcf-4a80-b44b-2137d8efddc3/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | _That's why I'm asking you to join us in supporting the remarkable Sweet Cheeks Diaper Bank. Sweet Cheeks has distributed over 2 million clean diapers in 2022 alone, an incredible way to decrease the cycle of poverty, help children in dire need and, often, just bring a smile to a baby's face. _ _No infant should suffer from diaper need in the richest country in the history of this planet._ [**_So, please join us in donating $5, $10, $25 or whatever you can afford to Sweet Cheeks Diaper Bank! As a reminder, this is a charitable donation, so any gift you give before 12am on New Year's Eve will count as a writeoff on your taxes for 2022._**](https://default.salsalabs.org/T3ad7269c-4190-430a-ba02-fabdc1c455c4/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) Thank you for your compassion,
---
Cliff

| | | About BlueAmp Action: We cannot sugarcoat the current situation. Right-wing authoritarianism, defeated by a worldwide coalition during WWII, was only in hibernation. Over the past decade it has infected democratic societies--including ours. It is as simple as it is terrifying: What we do now will define the future of our country and world. We at Blue Amp Action are dedicated to liberal, open societies, where reason and science are applied to solve our problems; where civil rights--including voting rights!--are protected for all. We fight for these values every day. Speaking up on our platforms, raising the voices and the political prospects of liberal pols and pundits. So we ask that you support our work, so we can continue to engage in this fight every moment of every day. So our kids grow up in democracy, not dystopia. Thank you for your support for all we do! Please follow Cliff Schecter on Twitter @cliffschecter, or subscribe to The Takedown with Cliff Schecter on YouTube, to keep up to date with this battle for the heart and soul of our country. — _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. name: AOTL
subject: What do you want 2023 to look like?
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE NATIONAL REDISTRICTING ACTION FUND
body:
There are two options. One is chaos.
|
---

* * *
**A.G. HOLDER:**
**“We’ve seen extreme Republicans go to great lengths to manipulate maps and overturn election results.** Now they are asking the Supreme Court to give unchecked power to state legislatures -- including those that remain heavily gerrymandered.”
* * *
Next year, the Supreme Court will decide _Moore v. Harper_. The case will directly impact the congressional map in North Carolina -- but extreme Republicans are already scheming how to leverage the Independent State Legislature (ISL) theory to further their anti-democracy agenda.

What do you want 2023 to look like?

**PRO-DEMOCRACY**
🗳 A fair and competitive congressional map in states like North Carolina
🗳 Voters advocating for redistricting reforms
🗳 State legislatures protecting and expanding access to voting

**ANTI-DEMOCRACY**
⚠️ Another decade of a gerrymandered maps like in North Carolina
⚠️ Extremists using ISL theory to challenge commission-drawn maps
⚠️ Gerrymandered state legislatures unilaterally restricting voting rights and installing election deniers to run federal elections

Now they are asking the Supreme Court to give unchecked power to state legislatures -- including those that remain heavily gerrymandered.” Next year, the Supreme Court will decide Moore v. Harper. The case will directly impact the congressional map in North Carolina -- but extreme Republicans are already scheming how to leverage the Independent State Legislature (ISL) theory to further their anti-democracy agenda. What do you want 2023 to look like? | Hello friend,
I am officially in the race for Florida’s House District 24 Special Election, and I urgently need your support.

_Here’s the situation..._

This Special Election will be over in just 9 weeks - the primary is March 7th, 2023.**And our first major deadline is Saturday, December 31st at midnight!**

That means I have just** 2 _ days_ **to demonstrate that I have what it takes to fight for Florida. Will you help me come out of the gate strong with an emergency donation of any amount today?
_**[→EMERGENCY Donate $1,000](https://clicks.aweber.com/y/ct/?l=4koMV&m=g1O1hKX3FQve0iI&b=Ypf71yExwz9m3nlp_PjIYQ)

%%First Name|Friend%%, whether you know me from the social media platform I founded to counter Big Tech, or from my past political activism, you know that I am a staunch conservative and I don’t back down.

Florida has come a long way under the exemplary leadership of Governor Ron DeSantis, but there’s still work to be done. **On issues like Pro-Life, Parental Rights, and the Second Amendment, Florida is lagging behind other states.** Help me change that!
**[FOR FLORIDA'S FUTURE](https://clicks.aweber.com/y/ct/?l=4koMV&m=g1O1hKX3FQve0iI&b=Ypf71yExwz9m3nlp_PjIYQ)**

name: Gabby Giffords
subject: This is the last time I’ll reach out to you this year
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Giffords PAC; not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee
body:
Time is running out, so I wanted to send you a message personally
#

Hi there, it’s Gabby.
I know my team has been in touch with you already about our end of year fundraising drive, but I wanted to reach out personally to explain why this deadline and your support are so incredibly crucial to Giffords PAC.
After tomorrow at midnight, we’ll close the books on our fundraising for the entire year and will have to report our numbers publicly. Both the total amount we’ve raised and the number of individual contributions we’ve received all year will be released, so this is an important milestone.
We’re still not quite where I hoped we’d be. But I know that with the strength of our community of supporters, we can get there.
I want to thank you from the bottom of my heart for supporting Giffords. It truly means a lot to me that you’re taking the time to read this.
A large part of our fundraising comes from the small dollar donations people make in response to emails like these. That’s why I’m asking if you can make today the day you make your first online contribution to Giffords PAC.
We couldn’t do our work to find solutions to end the gun violence epidemic and elect leaders with the courage to act without grassroots supporters.
[**So please, use this link to contribute $3 or any amount you can today.**](https://go.giffords.org/go/14457?c0=giffords-em-dec22.cgiIvC&refcode=em221230-1-15&t=3&akid=3094%2E3935704%2EYteV_n)
_If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly:_
| | **[Donate $3](https://go.giffords.org/go/17016?c0=giffords-em-dec22.jP9tfI&refcode=em221230-1-15&t=22&akid=3094%2E3935704%2EYteV_n)**
---

| **[Donate $25](https://go.giffords.org/go/17017?c0=giffords-em-dec22.cw1qcH&refcode=em221230-1-15&t=23&akid=3094%2E3935704%2EYteV_n)**

---
****
| **[Donate $100](https://go.giffords.org/go/17011?c0=giffords-em-dec22.EeXQyB&refcode=em221230-1-15&t=25&akid=3094%2E3935704%2EYteV_n)**
---

| | **[Donate $250](https://go.giffords.org/go/17012?c0=giffords-em-dec22.4PkA5L&refcode=em221230-1-15&t=26&akid=3094%2E3935704%2EYteV_n)**
---
****
| **[Other Amount](https://go.giffords.org/go/17015?c0=giffords-em-dec22.xfoOYP&refcode=em221230-1-15&t=27&akid=3094%2E3935704%2EYteV_n)**

This is the last email I’ll send this year. Thank you for your support of Giffords PAC, and I hope you have a safe, happy, and healthy New Year.
\- Gabby Giffords

Giffords PAC was started to support elected officials who step up to fight the gun violence epidemic. Thank you for standing with Gabby and the Giffords team.

name: Nikki Budzinski
subject: What an incredible year we’ve had together!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Nikki For Congress Nikki for Congress

Friend,

We have an urgent update from Team Lake.

An Arizona judge has ruled against Kari’s election lawsuit, but this trial was never the end all be all of this fight. This was always just the beginning.

**We will APPEAL, and we will bring this lawsuit all the way to the steps of the Supreme Court if need be.**

Kari’s finalizing our plans with the most formidable team of election lawyers this country has ever seen. [**BUT we’re going to need your help, Friend, if we’re going to be able to finish this fight.**](http://email.email.freethinkerdaily.com/c/eJx9ksFvgyAUxv8avWHkiVoPHrY126nHdkkvzas8K5miQ9hi__qBbbfbEgLhx8fjgw-qeVGC4CLnRSxrAcjbJlY1pAAcspTnGU-zROKGNtQiVQUHBIhESgOqPmkNke2U_iAj_XxJmnGIu1pIxA3PUzrLCkQlgHjKoch5eq6gKjdxX3fWTnOUPUXw6ttMjTOUfCttSIYigeEXMTTqOmpkrdOSKd14fvMGFTsbwg-lL36QbpJoiY3LFCRR9mpCReNXo2xrjaMIiuE0TlbpP-DscBpIKjd4Zuhr7tA8-Dw605Dnt5Irs2SC8hSc3Y0FX94WwytrxF3W4DChuoSD_vH6EI_akrb_a9d7sdZKvwmH0a36LPezk-6VDDfC85O25pBPK-yU9I-7xWM1k9g9x6aW07fqezUXpQ_vsoYXsvJWe9UudVQ-RwDrNh9viAWczQ643-_Mwt6Pn-wtP8AlLMKL7-0y0a_yXiRZ_0Sg5fYH51nB6Q)

This judge may not have ruled in our favor, but we have faith in the American justice system. It’s not too late to make things right.

[**According to our team, if every one of our supporters can contribute just $10 to Kari Lake’s fight to secure our elections and ensure the disaster that happened in Arizona is NOT repeated, our team will have the funds needed to bring this case to the Supreme Court.**](http://email.email.freethinkerdaily.com/c/eJx9kk1PtDAUhX8N7EroLR8zCxb6TtTNJG7UxM3k2l6gGSi8pXWCv94WR92ZEMh9enp64EANr2ooeFHyKlVNAchbmeoGcgAOIuel4LnIFO5oRy3SvuKAAEmR04h6yFpL5HptzmRVmNdMTmPaN4UQkIu8bDmWaif5264uy6rCYC8Ba0yHpnduXhJxk8BduBaS3lJ20caSiiaR4TsxtPpjMshabxTTRgb-lQ327M0SnrXpwkP5WaEjNq1zlCTizkZHG1YTcXDWUwLVeJpmp80v8G48jaS0HwOz9L70aL_5MnkrKfAvy405slF5ismuwWKuEIvhB5PFVSZxnFF38aA_sn6LJ-PIuL-123ux1qmwCcfJb3pRhulkBq3CcLy5nNvH7uH-uMFeq_BxD_i6X6g43qa2UfNFD4NeqjqU123lxa5C1EG3a5PUtwnAti3UG2sB78QzPj0d7cpeXv-z-_IZurgI_8LdrTP9KK8m2fZPRFofPgFABMHb)

clean_body: 🚨 🚨 🚨 🚨 🚨 🚨 | Friend, We have an urgent update from Team Lake. An Arizona judge has ruled against Kari’s election lawsuit, but this trial was never the end all be all of this fight. This was always just the beginning. We will APPEAL, and we will bring this lawsuit all the way to the steps of the Supreme Court if need be. Kari’s finalizing our plans with the most formidable team of election lawyers this country has ever seen. This judge may not have ruled in our favor, but we have faith in the American justice system. It’s not too late to make things right. | Best, Team Lake Paid for by Save Arizona Fund Inc.

* You will submit an Oregon tax return for 2022.
* You make less than $75,000 (individual) or $150,000 (couple filing jointly).

name: Adam Schiff
subject: It’s about to get ugly fast
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY SCHIFF FOR CONGRESS
body:
[ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/62?t=1001&akid=1524%2E5257348%2EqW4suL ]Adam Schiff for Congress

Derek, it’s about to get ugly, and fast, when Kevin McCarthy and
the Republican Party take the House majority next Tuesday.

[ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1312?c0=dec2022_abs_em.XWEzUA&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_GEN_EOQ_AC_20221230_AM1_V1_S1_VSP&email=&t=1004&akid=1524%2E5257348%2EqW4suL ]That’s why it’s so important that we build the resources now — ahead of
our final 2022 FEC deadline at midnight tomorrow — so that our campaign is
prepared to respond to the GOP’s attacks. Can you contribute?

If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your
donation will go through immediately:

[ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1015?c0=dec2022_abs_em.1CmCse&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_GEN_EOQ_AC_20221230_AM1_V1_S1_VSP&email=&t=1005&akid=1524%2E5257348%2EqW4suL ]EXPRESS DONATE $10

[ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1016?c0=dec2022_abs_em.IT0Q1v&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_GEN_EOQ_AC_20221230_AM1_V1_S1_VSP&email=&t=1006&akid=1524%2E5257348%2EqW4suL ]EXPRESS DONATE $25

[ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1017?c0=dec2022_abs_em.pdjp7M&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_GEN_EOQ_AC_20221230_AM1_V1_S1_VSP&email=&t=1007&akid=1524%2E5257348%2EqW4suL ]EXPRESS DONATE $50

[ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1018?c0=dec2022_abs_em.n-QQjE&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_GEN_EOQ_AC_20221230_AM1_V1_S1_VSP&email=&t=1008&akid=1524%2E5257348%2EqW4suL ]EXPRESS DONATE $100

[ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1019?c0=dec2022_abs_em.26VRxt&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_GEN_EOQ_AC_20221230_AM1_V1_S1_VSP&email=&t=1009&akid=1524%2E5257348%2EqW4suL ]EXPRESS DONATE $250

[ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1014?c0=dec2022_abs_em.lAzlNJ&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_GEN_EOQ_AC_20221230_AM1_V1_S1_VSP&email=&t=1010&akid=1524%2E5257348%2EqW4suL ]ANOTHER AMOUNT

Removing me from the Intel Committee is just the start.

McCarthy has put absolutely everything on the table.

Endless investigations into the President’s family. Articles of
impeachment against President Biden and his Cabinet officials. Whatever
Marjorie Taylor Greene and Matt Gaetz ask of him. Literally whatever they
ask.

It’s all on the table.

There’s no line McCarthy won’t cross in his vain pursuit of power at all
costs.

[ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1312?c0=dec2022_abs_em.XWEzUA&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_GEN_EOQ_AC_20221230_AM1_V1_S1_VSP&email=&t=1011&akid=1524%2E5257348%2EqW4suL ]Derek, the public report we file with the Federal Election
Commission after tomorrow’s midnight fundraising deadline officially kicks
off our campaign to take back the House majority from McCarthy. Anything,
and I mean anything you can contribute helps us get closer to that goal —
chip in $10 or whatever you can spare before midnight.

I’ve said all along that Kevin McCarthy was not fit to be Speaker.

He’s not even fit to represent his own district.

And with him leading House Republicans, he represents a real danger to the
democracy we’ve spent the last two years repairing from the harm Trump
caused.

It’s up to us to fight back, so thanks for doing what you can to support
our effort.

— Adam

150 E. Olive Ave., Suite 208 Burbank CA, 91502

[ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/60?t=1002&akid=1524%2E5257348%2EqW4suL ]www.adamschiff.com | [ mailto:contact@adamschiff.com ]contact@adamschiff.com | [ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/64?t=1003&akid=1524%2E5257348%2EqW4suL ]Privacy Policy

PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY SCHIFF FOR CONGRESS

You can unsubscribe from this mailing list at any time:
http://act.adamschiff.com/cms/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?akid=1524%2E5257348%2EqW4suL

Can you contribute? If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: [ ]EXPRESS DONATE $10 [ ]EXPRESS DONATE $25 [ ]EXPRESS DONATE $50 [ ]EXPRESS DONATE $100 [ ]EXPRESS DONATE $250 [ ]ANOTHER AMOUNT Removing me from the Intel Committee is just the start. McCarthy has put absolutely everything on the table. Endless investigations into the President’s family. Articles of impeachment against President Biden and his Cabinet officials. Whatever Marjorie Taylor Greene and Matt Gaetz ask of him. Literally whatever they ask. It’s all on the table. There’s no line McCarthy won’t cross in his vain pursuit of power at all costs. [ ]Derek, the public report we file with the Federal Election Commission after tomorrow’s midnight fundraising deadline officially kicks off our campaign to take back the House majority from McCarthy. Anything, and I mean anything you can contribute helps us get closer to that goal — chip in $10 or whatever you can spare before midnight. I’ve said all along that Kevin McCarthy was not fit to be Speaker. He’s not even fit to represent his own district. And with him leading House Republicans, he represents a real danger to the democracy we’ve spent the last two years repairing from the harm Trump caused. It’s up to us to fight back, so thanks for doing what you can to support our effort. — Adam 150 E. Olive Ave., Suite 208 Burbank CA, 91502 [ ]www.adamschiff.com | [ mailto:contact@adamschiff.com ]contact@adamschiff.com | [ ]Privacy Policy PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY SCHIFF FOR CONGRESS You can unsubscribe from this mailing list at any time:

name: Gene Karpinski, LCV
subject: Your LCV support: PENDING. Response needed. Please open today.
body:
__Time is running out to support our campaign for further climate action. |
---

| [](https://links.messages.lcv.org/els/v2/L6b_)
Peter -- Real quick before you sign off for the weekend: We still need your help to reach our $25,000 year-end fundraising goal.

**We 're already looking at a busy 2023.** In an effort to please their Corporate Polluter and Big Oil backers, anti-environment House Republicans are expected to IMMEDIATELY make a play to revive Keystone XL, expand drilling, and gut the National Environmental Policy Act.****

We're gearing up to fight back against House Republicans' dirty energy agenda. We're getting ready to win more state- and local-level clean energy progress through equitable and just implementation of the Inflation Reduction Act. And, we're campaigning relentlessly for President Biden to use executive action to advance climate progress and environmental justice, by:
* Declaring a climate emergency,
* Safeguarding public health and our waterways, and
* Setting ambitious climate standards for new and existing methane gas and coal-fired power plants, and new cars and trucks, among many other priorities.

But we need your help to start 2023 off with momentum on our side, Peter. [Please make a gift today to help us reach our goal -- even $5 helps. Together, we can raise $25,000 before December 31 to fight climate change and environmental injustice in the new year.](https://links.messages.lcv.org/els/v2/L6b_)

As soon as Congress comes back in January, our policy team will be on Capitol Hill fighting against the dirty energy agenda that House Republicans are planning to roll out. At the same time, we need to keep up our pressure campaign for executive action from the White House, all while ensuring effective implementation of the Inflation Reduction Act.

**But the truth is, resources are tight right now. We need your help.**

****_Now is not the time to ease up_ -- the next few months are going to set the tone for the future of climate action and environmental justice from the Biden-Harris administration. We need to continue cranking up the pressure so we can continue to make progress and defeat the worsening attacks on the environment.

| | **
** **LCV CAMPAIGN FOR CLIMATE ACTION**

_**Deadline:**_ Saturday, December 31, 2022
_**Fundraising Goal:**_ **NOT MET**

_**Email:**_ %%EMAIL ADDRESS%%
_**Your contribution:**_ [Give $5 now >>](https://links.messages.lcv.org/els/v2/L6b_) |
---|---|---

We can block Republicans' dirty energy agenda. And we can do it at the same time we EXPAND clean energy, PROTECT our water and air, and ADVANCE environmental justice.****

[If everyone reading this email gave just $5 right now, we'd hit our goal in minutes. Help give our campaign the resources it needs to win. We can do it -- and win -- but we need your support. Please give today.](https://links.messages.lcv.org/els/v2/L6b_)

Thanks for helping us reach our goal, Peter. From all of us at LCV, I hope you have a great weekend ringing in the New Year.

Onward,

Gene Karpinski
President
League of Conservation Voters

---

| This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If this isn't the best email address at which to reach you, [update your contact information](https://www.pages08.net/leagueofconservationvoters/forms/ContactInformation?spMailingID=9043166&spUserID=NjA3MDU0MTAzNTc3S0&spJobID=MTY0MDQ3MTE5OQS2&spReportId=MTY0MDQ3MTE5OQS2). [Click here to unsubscribe](https://www.pages08.net/leagueofconservationvoters/forms/Unsubscribe?spMailingID=9043166&spUserID=NjA3MDU0MTAzNTc3S0&spJobID=MTY0MDQ3MTE5OQS2&spReportId=MTY0MDQ3MTE5OQS2) from our supporter list. Send us any comments, criticisms, or feedback [here](mailto:feedback@lcv.org), or just reply to this email! Thanks for your support.

[740 15th St NW, 7th Floor
Washington, DC 20005
202-785-8683](https://links.messages.lcv.org/els/v2/x_) |

Peter,
We hope you've had a wonderful holiday season and are looking forward to the new year.
We wanted to quickly reach out and thank you for all your support in 2022. Because of you, we were able to raise over $45,000 in support of Beto O'Rourke and other hard-fighting Texas Democrats.
Although the election didn't go as we'd hoped, we're proud of the work we did together, and we're excited for what the future holds.
(Remember... Ted Cruz is up for re-election in 2024...)
As the year comes to a close, we need to know: are you still all in on the fight to save Texas?

---
Friend,

I will be the first to tell you there were plenty of times I thought about giving up and throwing in the towel.

**At this point -- that's just not an option. **

Our case proved, without a doubt, that not everything was as it seemed during Arizona's November election.

Now, the far-left political operatives and Fake News media are hoping this recent ruling will end my fight once and for all.

**They are dead wrong. I 'm not going to stop fighting.**

I promise you -- I will appeal this case and continue our struggle to secure our election until my very last breath.

**However, I need to know -- do I have your support?**

| [**YES, YOU HAVE MY SUPPORT!** ](https://links.conservativelibertywire.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=65e6639eba7b4bd4abff0fe3016f0e1a&_e=GezFdK5OZqxMP_vdwlacHQAlVNjI5Qmu2PHTfEjcJrb_ah1nbEAwqmvGnMpcOHyxyGPUbz3V8iEK9kWJM8J8Usm-Brnth8sq0uCqfoXjtXRiI_SFOrAW65s7u5g2d94GhNs1eqUnU6E1vAs3jcA_GtO8l_oH9QeqcE_FaM0xQR5g4KKppOCjXo4jRdC8lecbQSrN4TGKKRvtuF_AnHcenzzZt6GwHoXFfN7dRzzKRqaLwzJ3fegJxSOPkJtI-AAj5hYt_uOhQPMukbhpa91K9UXit4CN310xrM68wJ2NY5OSb31sAtV_27B88LhLAgirFSmOuqcoUib-JoQTuw7-ql8kOZX0gAVB46m-WTTss3bLkckITx9gvuXm2NO3OABhSxIVN4TpMBGLNuRuJZtlzhffSQXoYFeUppjCVColS_v3XgyGDboHdORqA-N-JArHT-wsNk0l3o9b84tYAsHmYTs6Z0vczwkzSqdeC_-osj-v5-BheZ8k_sNRsPSydlBzUYlPcGU3evFiGoMp7JxLIowFUrZbZNV3F_iwXVOSStvruVjEK9QzZqgHXF3cC8RN)
---

[**I can 't do this alone, Friend**](https://links.conservativelibertywire.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=65e6639eba7b4bd4abff0fe3016f0e1a&_e=GezFdK5OZqxMP_vdwlacHQAlVNjI5Qmu2PHTfEjcJrb_ah1nbEAwqmvGnMpcOHyxyGPUbz3V8iEK9kWJM8J8Usm-Brnth8sq0uCqfoXjtXRiI_SFOrAW65s7u5g2d94GhNs1eqUnU6E1vAs3jcA_GtO8l_oH9QeqcE_FaM0xQR5g4KKppOCjXo4jRdC8lecbQSrN4TGKKRvtuF_AnHcenzzZt6GwHoXFfN7dRzzKRqaLwzJ3fegJxSOPkJtI-AAj5hYt_uOhQPMukbhpa91K9UXit4CN310xrM68wJ2NY5OSb31sAtV_27B88LhLAgirFSmOuqcoUib-JoQTuw7-ql8kOZX0gAVB46m-WTTss3bLkckITx9gvuXm2NO3OABhSxIVN4TpMBGLNuRuJZtlznw4hft2Uoo6_nrU_INwXL63LAK4DWrP0fKokf-CWhf_odrT8y9cu4TA725kmxqWK0ttX2MZNbo_uA-lInilZyNDsIcpuZ7wRfK-dgr0v0fvsguSR2Bqc5f63bxmJuEbOcEOrjmcT-hzO8qex4jHYOwJpWOBAGvI4JT1VbEOnzgY)

Our case proved, without a doubt, that not everything was as it seemed during Arizona's November election. Now, the far-left political operatives and Fake News media are hoping this recent ruling will end my fight once and for all. They are dead wrong. I 'm not going to stop fighting. I promise you -- I will appeal this case and continue our struggle to secure our election until my very last breath. However, I need to know -- do I have your support? | Best, Kari Lake Paid for by Save Arizona Fund Inc. Sent to Conservative Liberty Wire subscribers. 625 First Street #183, Alexandria, VA 22314

Friend, In 2018, Russian authorities arrested U.S. Marine Paul Whelan, wrongly accusing him of being a spy. They convicted him of espionage and sentenced him to 16 years in prison. _Things took a turn for the worse recently ..._ The Biden Administration had the opportunity to secure Whelan's release in December. Instead, the White House agreed to release a violent Russian arms dealer in exchange for WNBA player Brittney Griner, who was convicted of possessing marijuana in Russia.

Peter, what a year we’ve had!

Despite setbacks — _recovering from COVID‐19, facing unprecedented threats to our democracy and our rights_ — we made a tremendous amount of progress.

[**So as we look toward the New Year and a Republican-controlled U.S. House, I hope you’ll continue to stand with me. Please Peter, can I count on you to chip in $3 toward our $15,000 year‐end goal to keep this up? »**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/56648660/385606042/234291150?amount=3&refcode=MTFC_20221230_EMDF_E1_T0_AC&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDM2MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlYmE3NjVlYS04NDg4LWVkMTEtOWQ3YS0wMDIyNDgzMmU2MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=xhiuvX97OThPkrqAw4FuH7XW5uvroiiNKOAFZWCCwZg=) | [RUSH $3 TO SUPPORT PROGRESSIVE MARK TAKANO »](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/56648661/385606043/234291150?amount=3&refcode=MTFC_20221230_EMDF_E1_T0_AC&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDM2MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlYmE3NjVlYS04NDg4LWVkMTEtOWQ3YS0wMDIyNDgzMmU2MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=xhiuvX97OThPkrqAw4FuH7XW5uvroiiNKOAFZWCCwZg=)

Peter, I know the news can get exhausting and negative, so let me remind you of some incredible accomplishments we made this year:

**President Biden signed my veteran health care legislation, the PACT ACT, into law.** Now, thousands of veterans will get the care they deserve and need.

**The Respect for Marriage Act is now law.** As an openly LGBTQ+ member of Congress, this means the world to me.

**We won re-election!** We denied an extreme Trump Republican from taking over this seat — and it was all thanks to people like you supporting this movement.

But Peter, there is still much to work on...
❌ _Abortion is illegal across half the country, millions of Americans are barely scraping by, and extremist Republicans barely won control of the House._ ❌

clean_body: we made a tremendous amount of progress... | = Peter, what a year we’ve had! Despite setbacks — recovering from COVID‐19, facing unprecedented threats to our democracy and our rights — we made a tremendous amount of progress. = | = Peter, I know the news can get exhausting and negative, so let me remind you of some incredible accomplishments we made this year: President Biden signed my veteran health care legislation, the PACT ACT, into law. Now, thousands of veterans will get the care they deserve and need. The Respect for Marriage Act is now law. As an openly LGBTQ+ member of Congress, this means the world to me. We won re-election! We denied an extreme Trump Republican from taking over this seat — and it was all thanks to people like you supporting this movement. But Peter, there is still much to work on... ❌ Abortion is illegal across half the country, millions of Americans are barely scraping by, and extremist Republicans barely won control of the House. ❌ = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: = = = Thank you, — Mark Takano | | Paid for by Mark Takano For Congress P.O. name: Joe Shepherd, President of United Rural Democrats
subject: Our New Year’s Resolution
disclaimer_text: Paid for by United Rural Democrats](https://click
body:
We’re ready for 2023 | | | Hi Peter, With the midterm elections in our rearview, we’re preparing for the next election cycle and how we can help elect more rural Democrats. We’ve had time to relax, unwind, and recharge our batteries. Now, we’re ready for whatever 2023 throws our way. And, we want to keep you in the loop since your support made all of our successes possible. The midterms demonstrated that grassroots organizing and forming connections with rural voters makes all the difference. Our hard work of traveling across the country and _actually_ connecting with people helped rural Democrats win big in 2022. We also saw a few rural Dems lose because they didn’t have enough support and resources from our party. But we know that with so much at stake, every race and vote counts. And if we run up the margins in these hard to win races, we'll eventually prevail. Remember the story of Dodge City I emailed about earlier this month? So, in 2023, we’re implementing a new strategy that prioritizes voters. We will continue our consulting work with campaigns, organizations, and politicians to ensure rural Americans are not left behind. As always, we’re working to cut them in on the deal. **[We’ll share more with you in the new year, but until then, will you support our work by making a monthly contribution commitment?](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953xNG6r6b6yi4Uz9vCRXD2GclY3l1Sg3yIgAa7xFIV6TGLFwuovjhbiiB9VcZRM2Z0vis4S6zWhbFJxO0KoJA5Zwo2Oz6snJjLyPKFrxHJPZUWyWbLKqHw9fi5NQUPsoYdF5w_crCkoAfXDLUjoICqguXZVmq28LibVe-1_sZjGJFg9oC0DrmtEeqTuDc0UpIFpUxuCGUa00pzSuJkEWIAyg7vLGK2y2fG4sEgWtkeqUsict7U6R5SIOCQcHCuK6UceBx8nXR-GjqVaBIECGeylYIl9R3Oa2-qK3IGhRj2Y2SIbdrL1p7n7K_T0rKbsyQ-AFMZaCUeaNhF6P35qFwUNpmrwssVKMNyBr_XniUaXjDw/3sf/L04Ps30cSNOcx26_XjSB4w/h0/T3fda9Yfd-mnWyA5kl0FRX10_oO61ADB0TwFQrQyykI)** **[SUPPORT RURAL DEMOCRATS MONTHLY](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953xNG6r6b6yi4Uz9vCRXD2GclY3l1Sg3yIgAa7xFIV6TGLFwuovjhbiiB9VcZRM2Z0vis4S6zWhbFJxO0KoJA5ZwfXzKhhS8uOUtXhEl-3f4k1UkXXE52eyYKy0mu9kKqEwiPuKn5SGk-43I0uct5yWikQl0IyvJ6MgNh6qN1Wemtlu_iHwfQAoI--1ndg7ZqPWCfxPjNtY-Bq_8b1QsU6m2BXP4IWjnFx_BsvZjbwYrGG3mczI9j0eWyEIIYcH7tOJmV30aJujZjNNz0PDyqo2mwGjSogEOV8uv8fmEpzmBbY2XKwlafnNMfSckPUOuaaaxJ3p3W9LjGVsu7jNa3mUj3twKMGCyMCCPqP7PQqEh0Q/3sf/L04Ps30cSNOcx26_XjSB4w/h1/BlVZs0-LWU0zs0YaNlKH1PMIS0AYbLEpZ8mRCPxiups)** Happy new year, Peter! \- Joe Shepherd, President of United Rural Democrats

name: NDRC Supporter Update
subject: 2022 midterm recap for dpwillis67@gmail.com
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the National Democratic Redistricting Committee, Kelly Burton, President
body:
we have good news and bad news
|
---

Team, now that the 2022 midterm election is officially over, we want to give our top grassroots supporters a quick overview of what our fight for fair maps helped accomplish.

✅ **First, the good news:** In states like Colorado, Pennsylvania, North Carolina, Minnesota, and Michigan, our work helped secure some of the fairest maps we’ve seen in decades. As a result, Democrats:
* Won full control of state government in Michigan and Minnesota, flipped the Pennsylvania House, and blocked North Carolina Republicans from winning a veto-proof majority.
* Won tough congressional seats in Colorado and North Carolina where Republicans were heavily favored to win – because these new districts were created as a result of fair redistricting processes.
* Re-elected vulnerable House Democrats in Michigan and Pennsylvania.

[**_Help the NDRC fight for more victories like these! Donate now >>>_**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/56610588/385546607/-1417996814?amount=30&refcode=20221219.1a_527&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjUxNjI2NTA3LTViODgtZWQxMS05ZDdhLTAwMjI0ODMyZTYxMSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=UDJ3lR8JX7o2-LmZnpq7aY_ZWblTUacimEsbXMqhoq4=) ❌ **But there’s also bad news:** In four states – Alabama, Georgia, Louisiana, and Ohio – voters were forced to participate in elections with unconstitutional maps. [**_That’s why the NDRC is continuing to fight to hold map-manipulating politicians accountable, help elect fair-minded judges, defend voting rights, and more. Will you pitch in now to help us keep going?_**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/56610590/385546608/-1417996814?amount=30&refcode=20221219.1a_527&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjUxNjI2NTA3LTViODgtZWQxMS05ZDdhLTAwMjI0ODMyZTYxMSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=UDJ3lR8JX7o2-LmZnpq7aY_ZWblTUacimEsbXMqhoq4=) | _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_

clean_body: we have good news and bad news | Team, now that the 2022 midterm election is officially over, we want to give our top grassroots supporters a quick overview of what our fight for fair maps helped accomplish. ✅ First, the good news: In states like Colorado, Pennsylvania, North Carolina, Minnesota, and Michigan, our work helped secure some of the fairest maps we’ve seen in decades. As a result, Democrats: Won full control of state government in Michigan and Minnesota, flipped the Pennsylvania House, and blocked North Carolina Republicans from winning a veto-proof majority. Won tough congressional seats in Colorado and North Carolina where Republicans were heavily favored to win – because these new districts were created as a result of fair redistricting processes. Re-elected vulnerable House Democrats in Michigan and Pennsylvania. = ❌ But there’s also bad news: In four states – Alabama, Georgia, Louisiana, and Ohio – voters were forced to participate in elections with unconstitutional maps. = | If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: = | = = | = = | = Attorney General Eric Holder said, “Wherever Democrats can fairly compete, they can win. The election results underscore that.” He’s 100% right, team! In states where our movement helped secure fair maps, Democrats defied history and won tough races. Now, we need to keep fighting for fairness – state by state, and all across the country. = – The NDRC Team = = Since 2017, the NDRC has executed a comprehensive redistricting strategy that shifts the redistricting power, creating fair districts where Democrats can compete. Our victories have been made possible by our strong community of grassroots supporters. = If you want to receive periodic updates from the NDRC on our fight for fair maps, text MAPS to 36787. Peter Willis,

Since our founding, Our Revolution has lived
on the frontlines of the fight to deliver results for the working class by
organizing to elect progressives and advance popular policies.

In 2022, we delivered the critical margins of victory for Senator Warnock,
Senator Fetterman, and nine new progressive members of Congress!

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/8172?source=OR_EM_FR_25SP_20221230_1_5&t=1011&akid=3840%2E5885482%2EwITyHk ]Now, we are leading the fight to defeat Kyrsten Sinema in 2024 because
early investments are critical to outmaneuvering corporate power. Can we
count on you to contribute before our end-of-year deadline as a founding
donor of our Defeat Sinema Fund?

If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation to
Our Revolution will process immediately.

Our big wins in Georgia and PA mean Senate committees will be in
Democratic control — giving Bernie Sanders the ability to investigate
corporate greed in his new role as Chair of the HELP Committee, and the
establishment is striking back.

Big money floods the pockets of shills like Sinema and authoritarian
Republicans who will subvert democracy for personal gain, but we have each
other. Our movement is growing, there is important work to be done in the
year to come, and we need to hit the ground running.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/8172?source=OR_EM_FR_25SP_20221230_1_5&t=1018&akid=3840%2E5885482%2EwITyHk ]Please help fund our organizing in 2023 by pitching in before our
end-of-year deadline!

In solidarity,

Our Revolution

name: Laura Nevitt,  Keystone Progress
subject: To do before the New Year.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Keystone Progress Education Fund, www
body:
https://www.kpef.info [https://www.kpef.info]

Dear Friend,

Now is the time to invest in progressive politics here in Pennsylvania. We have a chance to mobilize and grow our movement and to make sure that our shared values are represented in Harrisburg, in our local, municipal offices., and by our local judges. But we need your help.

Please give us that push we need before the year ends and support the work we're doing on the ground to strengthen Pennsylvania's progressive movement.

name: Team DPVA
subject: Auld Lang Syne, Democrats
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Democratic Party of Virginia, www
body:
Democrats have had a busy 2022.

|
---
Derek, As the year comes to an end, we have plenty of Democratic accomplishments to reflect on. Enshrining marriage equality in federal law, passing meaningful gun safety reforms, and all that was encompassed by the Inflation Reduction Act – **Democrats have been hard at work.** But with a busy legislative session ahead here in the Commonwealth, there’s still a lot of work ahead for Democrats. Glenn Youngkin and the VA GOP are pushing their far-right ban on abortion, and the Democratic majority in the Senate is all that stands in their way. That’s why it’s so important that Democrats in SD-07 elect Aaron Rouse on January 10. **[Can we count on you to chip in and give Democrats the resources they need to expand our Senate majority next month?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/56649982/385610598/-1925813607?refcode=dpva-em-221230-eom5&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84NDIzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2MjFhYTI2ZS04ODg4LWVkMTEtOWQ3YS0wMDIyNDgzMmU2MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=tFfVRUrp3t2OjSn5n2CEdts4uymRSyrNMtYtik3zkos=)** | _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_

clean_body: Democrats have had a busy 2022. | Derek, As the year comes to an end, we have plenty of Democratic accomplishments to reflect on. Enshrining marriage equality in federal law, passing meaningful gun safety reforms, and all that was encompassed by the Inflation Reduction Act – Democrats have been hard at work. But with a busy legislative session ahead here in the Commonwealth, there’s still a lot of work ahead for Democrats. Glenn Youngkin and the VA GOP are pushing their far-right ban on abortion, and the Democratic majority in the Senate is all that stands in their way. That’s why it’s so important that Democrats in SD-07 elect Aaron Rouse on January 10. = | If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: ********=****** ******=****** ******=****** ******=****** With December and 2022 both coming to an end, it’s more important than ever that we hit our monthly goal in order to elect Aaron Rouse next month. = Team DPVA =****** =******** [content goes here] Democratic Party of Virginia PO Box 448 Richmond, VA 23218 United States Paid for by the Democratic Party of Virginia, www.vademocrats.org. name: Cheri Beasley
subject: Maggie Hassan needs our support before the end of the year
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Cheri Beasley for North Carolina
body:
I hope you and your loved one are having a restful holiday season
#
Hi there -
I hope you and your loved one are having a restful holiday season. I’ve spent a lot of time reflecting and making up for lost time with Curt and our boys — Matthew, Thomas and Stanley — after a busy campaign schedule!
But today, I am reaching out with a special favor to ask. I got a note from Senator Maggie Hassan (below) that her campaign has a few bills they still need to pay from their victorious campaign. They ran to win, and I told her we could help because we don’t want her to have to focus on fundraising when the important work of the new Congress begins.
She and her team always had my back and pitched in tremendously to support our efforts, so I’m hoping you will read Sen. Hassan’s note below and [**consider contributing to her campaign before we all close the books on 2022 with Saturday’s deadline.**](https://act.cheribeasley.com/go/2567?c0=cb-em-hassan.aRhz2v&refcode=em221230-1-mat1&t=1&akid=2277%2E357359%2EYnAwFL)
Thank you so much for your support.
\- Cheri Beasley
Senator Maggie Hassan’s email is below.
* * *
Hi there,
It is the honor of my lifetime to serve in the Senate. Working to make life better for Granite Staters is something that deserves my full attention.
That’s why I’m sending you this email. Our (victorious!) campaign has some debt to pay off, and the amount we owe will be publicly reported at the end of the year. [**If you could give $3 right now to help us pay it down so that I can continue focusing my energy on serving in the Senate, I’d be very grateful.**](https://act.cheribeasley.com/go/2567?c0=cb-em-hassan.aRhz2v&refcode=em221230-1-mat1&t=2&akid=2277%2E357359%2EYnAwFL)
The backstory is this:
GOP spending against us SKYROCKETED in the last few weeks of the campaign. Polls tightened to within a point or two, and pundits doubted whether we could win.
My team was laser-focused on making strategic decisions that would allow us to win. We made last-minute investments with the information we had, hoping that the funding we needed would come through.
Unfortunately, all of our funding didn’t come through and now my campaign has a bit of a hole to fill to get our last bills paid.
[**Will you please give $3 or as much as you can to my campaign to help us retire our debt? Every dollar you give helps me spend less time fundraising and more time fighting for hardworking families, and I’m grateful for anything you can give.**](https://act.cheribeasley.com/go/2567?c0=cb-em-hassan.aRhz2v&refcode=em221230-1-mat1&t=3&akid=2277%2E357359%2EYnAwFL)
Thank you so much.
With every good wish,
Maggie Hassan
**[DONATE](https://act.cheribeasley.com/go/2567?c0=cb-em-hassan.aRhz2v&refcode=em221230-1-mat1&refcode2=2277.357359.YnAwFL&t=4&akid=2277%2E357359%2EYnAwFL)**

Paid For By Cheri Beasley for North Carolina
Cheri Beasley for North Carolina
PO Box 28778
Raleigh, NC 27611
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If you want to receive fewer emails, you may [submit this form to get only our most important updates](https://act.cheribeasley.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/?t=5&akid=2277%2E357359%2EYnAwFL). Emails like this are a critical tool for our campaign's fundraising and organizing efforts, but if you need to, you can [unsubscribe here](https://act.cheribeasley.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=6&akid=2277%2E357359%2EYnAwFL). If you would like to support Cheri's campaign for the U.S. Senate in North Carolina, please consider [making a donation](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/beasley-em-2021?refcode=em-footer&t=7&refcode2=2277_357359_YnAwFL&akid=2277%2E357359%2EYnAwFL) today to help us make history.

name: Vice President Kamala Harris
subject: I’ll keep this short, Derek.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Democratic National Committee, **[www
body:
Your donation will help elect more Democrats up and down the ballot nationwide. | |
---
Together, Democrats have made significant strides this year. We protected our Senate majority. We lost fewer seats in the House than in any Democratic president’s first midterm election in at least 40 years. And we passed critical legislation that puts workers and families first. **I know for a fact that we could not have come this far without your support, Derek. Thank you.** **I also know there’s still more work to do to continue building a better America.** From protecting the right to vote to organizing across the country, the DNC is laying the groundwork to elect Democrats up and down the ballot nationwide in the years ahead. They are relying on your support ahead of their important fundraising deadline tomorrow. **[I am asking you to step up for the first time this year in celebration of our victories and the promise of a brighter tomorrow: Will you contribute $7 to the DNC before tomorrow's fundraising deadline?](https://click.act.democrats.org/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953wQKWnG_Y5cKFA2Q0-EPQM0xTQcmp7F_CrPGvMV_QmSnGr2wmz2xe5iPz-Qk-lBVn-bUKqX9dZk5narE5ohTD_4v7MaAvjuPcgqdauMahStivO8YgdD2LadWlmiEWMAKO7jWHBQDuP1ab4aK4hlmBTS8P0IR8vGCu4V4W5nKCGJ9UiU4cEg-C7CWply4Ugt7gMm3sRT7LrW_ICizmVyePXQbDXQjMOqd3CXRTlPuzVVamzLAQzlAZ5bY8yBe8MdqUG-HrxgSJjx8ttbBeTthfaBetvX-TKC76lKf4MXOQA9zvaTPy3nq2YRkhl9O-dAkZYLGJXneysW9aTEuWl78WSIJaK6K2mpIUUs9gnr1akMFr1pdL2JTRkDCAd0qItGNW8KmZOQsYSSV9bszDZLR13AP3ajSDhhEODe1x0PYt0WdslyAW7jjWhKA2D0IBIF-dDc8VwViyUAWiJIfC6cJywp3hD9SpML5mBV_Pc8l6zB_SWfLIpmHiRFHmhjLf21hhhRLUM8uizp_w624KOHSAo6HXNCHVYAjbidbftQMKrwmQ/3sf/yWJ411RfSGikTj7rOUX8zw/h0/QN7ObhGTJ7GU7rKJaLOYOZI895LsnksdCYt6hHkic1o)**

clean_body: Your donation will help elect more Democrats up and down the ballot nationwide. | | Together, Democrats have made significant strides this year. We protected our Senate majority. We lost fewer seats in the House than in any Democratic president’s first midterm election in at least 40 years. And we passed critical legislation that puts workers and families first. I know for a fact that we could not have come this far without your support, Derek. Thank you. I also know there’s still more work to do to continue building a better America. From protecting the right to vote to organizing across the country, the DNC is laying the groundwork to elect Democrats up and down the ballot nationwide in the years ahead. They are relying on your support ahead of their important fundraising deadline tomorrow. Thank you for all that you do, Kamala Kamala Harris Vice President of the United States Kamala Harris: Derivative of Adam Schultz / Biden for President Photo. body:
As you know, the dark money flowing into Rhode Island from Kevin McCarthy’s SuperPAC skyrocketed in the last few weeks of our campaign.
[ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/56650337/385612093/-1862136002?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTcyMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmYTM3OTU2My04YTg4LWVkMTEtOWQ3YS0wMDIyNDgzMmU2MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=O1kw3HVOfkMCqe88l6gPpbGVdWJYMfwwv4extlrOdg4=&emci=08f65e88-7e88-ed11-9d7a-00224832e611&emdi=fa379563-8a88-ed11-9d7a-00224832e611&ceid=15384964)
Hi Peter,

As you know, the dark money flowing into Rhode Island from Kevin McCarthy’s SuperPAC skyrocketed in the last few weeks of our campaign. We were down in every poll and every pundit doubted we could win. But you stood by my side and together we stayed on the air with TV ads and put together a strong ground game to get out the vote. Our hard work paid off and we were victorious!

Unfortunately, we didn’t raise quite enough to keep up with the Republican dark money and we did end up with some final bills to pay.

If everyone receiving this email chipped in just $5, we would be able to pay our final bills and I can continue to focus my energy on lowering the cost of prescription drugs, investing in a clean energy economy, and standing up for fundamental rights like reproductive rights and the right to vote.

**[Will you chip in before the FEC end-of-year fundraising deadline to help me pay our final bills? I would be incredibly grateful.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/56650338/385612094/-688972559?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTcyMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmYTM3OTU2My04YTg4LWVkMTEtOWQ3YS0wMDIyNDgzMmU2MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=O1kw3HVOfkMCqe88l6gPpbGVdWJYMfwwv4extlrOdg4=)**

name: Team Durbin
subject: 2022’s last deadline
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Dick Durbin
body:
We need to head into the new year strong.
#
Peter,
We made history in 2022. For the first time since 1934, every incumbent Senator of the President’s party won re-election. Support from people like you helped make that possible.
This year has shown us that the only way we can stop extremism in its tracks and defend our democracy is by coming together as a united front.
**[Tomorrow night is the last end-of-quarter deadline of 2022. name: LTC (Ret.) Alexander Vindman
subject: Humbly asking you to contribute $3 to VoteVets today
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY VOTEVETS, VOTEVETS
body:
Please give me a chance to explain
#
Derek -
In just a moment, I am going to ask you to make a $3 contribution to VoteVets before their end-of-year fundraising drive comes to a close tomorrow at midnight.
If you know their work and that’s all you need to hear, please contribute using this link:
[**https://act.votevets.org/go/EOY-deadline**](https://act.votevets.org/go/2927?c0=vv-pac-em-dec22.pX32s6&refcode=em221230-1-15&t=1&akid=7252%2E3013755%2E6jGuq-)
Otherwise, let me explain:
In 2022, VoteVets ran one of the largest and most effective spending campaigns in the country. Their ads moved voters and helped save many House and Senate seats.
In 2022, VoteVets helped me raise more than $500,000 to assist the people of Ukraine after the humanitarian disaster caused by Russia’s illegal invasion. That’s money that made a difference in people’s lives.
In 2022, VoteVets helped win the passage of the PACT Act, getting veterans from several wars the care, treatment and benefits they earned through service.
Now we have divided Congress and a critical election ahead, and VoteVets will have an essential role in the fights ahead.
They can’t do their work without support from people like you. And as a Senior Advisor to VoteVets, I am humbly asking:
[**Can you contribute $3, or whatever you can afford today, to VoteVets before their end-of-year fundraising drive comes to a close tomorrow at midnight? I would not ask were it not so important.**](https://act.votevets.org/go/2927?c0=vv-pac-em-dec22.pX32s6&refcode=em221230-1-15&t=2&akid=7252%2E3013755%2E6jGuq-)
_If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly:_
| | **[Rush $10 now](https://act.votevets.org/go/2934?c0=vv-pac-em-dec22.eb_Obm&refcode=em221230-1-15&t=21&akid=7252%2E3013755%2E6jGuq-)**
---
****
| **[Rush $25 now](https://act.votevets.org/go/2935?c0=vv-pac-em-dec22.HCzzxr&refcode=em221230-1-15&t=22&akid=7252%2E3013755%2E6jGuq-)**

****
| | **[Rush $50 now](https://act.votevets.org/go/2928?c0=vv-pac-em-dec22.1Y-X16&refcode=em221230-1-15&t=23&akid=7252%2E3013755%2E6jGuq-)**
---
****
| **[Rush $100 now](https://act.votevets.org/go/2929?c0=vv-pac-em-dec22.ypdOja&refcode=em221230-1-15&t=24&akid=7252%2E3013755%2E6jGuq-)**
---
****
| | **[Rush $250 now](https://act.votevets.org/go/2930?c0=vv-pac-em-dec22.qsrf1M&refcode=em221230-1-15&t=25&akid=7252%2E3013755%2E6jGuq-)**
---
****
| **[Another Amount](https://act.votevets.org/go/2933?c0=vv-pac-em-dec22.8cNmpq&refcode=em221230-1-15&t=26&akid=7252%2E3013755%2E6jGuq-)**

****
Thank you, Derek. Your support means the world.
Alexander Vindman

Greetings friend!

When I left my house this morning, a blast of cool 19 degree wind blasted me in the face, tracing its way around my ears!

Talk about being caught off guard!

Then I realized...

I had my Tactical Balaclava around my neck...

I quickly pulled it up over my head and within seconds I was protected from that nasty NYC winter gust.

Let me tell you...

[Tactical Balaclavas](https://links.thegunpeople.com/a/1537/click/11081/6374453/f9c14dc330220fd6768a3c7ae9fbbe501578a3ac/d0403237706a1b977a7385614db62ad10c06fb82) are an absolute necessity for any outdoor enthusiast.

They’re made of durable, breathable material and designed to protect your head and neck from harsh weather. And they can be worn several different ways, depending on the style you’re going for.

They’re also a solid solution for protection against UV rays, bugs, and other outdoor annoyances.

I love mine and it goes with me almost all the time.

And right now, Gunner Gear has a steal running for the holidays...

[If you buy one balaclava, they’re giving away an additional 3 (THREE) for FREE! ](https://links.thegunpeople.com/a/1537/click/11081/6374453/f9c14dc330220fd6768a3c7ae9fbbe501578a3ac/d0403237706a1b977a7385614db62ad10c06fb82)

That’s unheard of and an untouchable deal!

For real, hurry up before they run out...

With the holidays here, tactical enthusiasts are snagging them up quick!

Don’t lose out, [go here now](https://links.thegunpeople.com/a/1537/click/11081/6374453/f9c14dc330220fd6768a3c7ae9fbbe501578a3ac/d0403237706a1b977a7385614db62ad10c06fb82) and get some for yourself and your friends.

All the best,

Donald J. Trump December 30th, 2022
The Democrats should have never done it, the Supreme Court should have never approved it, and it’s going to lead to horrible things for so many people. The great USA divide will now grow far worse. The Radical Left Democrats have weaponized everything, but remember, that is a dangerous two-way street! The “Trump” tax returns once again show how proudly successful I have been and how I have been able to use depreciation and various other tax deductions as an incentive for creating thousands of jobs and magnificent structures and enterprises.

Forbes: “Trump’s assets are worth an estimated $4.3 billion...The former president owns real stuff—mansions, golf courses, office buildings—that throw off real cash, even if his tax returns might suggest otherwise...The D.C. hotel opened in 2016 and, by 2018, Trump had already declared tax losses of $55.5 million there, according to the Times. Still, the property is worth something. One investor offered $175 million for it before the coronavirus decimated the hotel industry. The Trumps turned that offer down. Forbes now figures the hotel is worth closer to $168 million.”—Wrong, just sold the Hotel for almost $400 Million. Many of their other numbers are wrong too, but by even bigger proportions. But that’s O.K., being wrong doesn’t matter to the Fake News!

Trump December 30th, 2022 The Democrats should have never done it, the Supreme Court should have never approved it, and it’s going to lead to horrible things for so many people. The great USA divide will now grow far worse. The Radical Left Democrats have weaponized everything, but remember, that is a dangerous two-way street! The “Trump” tax returns once again show how proudly successful I have been and how I have been able to use depreciation and various other tax deductions as an incentive for creating thousands of jobs and magnificent structures and enterprises. Forbes: “Trump’s assets are worth an estimated $4.3 billion...The former president owns real stuff—mansions, golf courses, office buildings—that throw off real cash, even if his tax returns might suggest otherwise...The D.C. hotel opened in 2016 and, by 2018, Trump had already declared tax losses of $55.5 million there, according to the Times. Still, the property is worth something. One investor offered $175 million for it before the coronavirus decimated the hotel industry. The Trumps turned that offer down. Forbes now figures the hotel is worth closer to $168 million.”—Wrong, just sold the Hotel for almost $400 Million. Many of their other numbers are wrong too, but by even bigger proportions. But that’s O.K., being wrong doesn’t matter to the Fake News! | ---|--- Donald J.
