name: =?utf-8?Q?Catherine=20Engelbrecht=20|=20True=20the=20Vote?=
subject: True the Vote Year End
body:
Please consider making a tax-deductible donation for 2022 | | | |
---
| | Hi Friend \-
Happy New Year!
As we look ahead to 2023, [**there is still time to help True the Vote** ](https://truethevote.us8.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d9ab56570d4ecf7b7df9d4c25&id=936918c782&e=ad8cba7e98)stand strong in defense of voters’ rights. We face a formidable foe - a political machine that requires our complacency to retain its power. Over the past several years, it has become clear that there is a larger scheme afoot, one that intends to fundamentally change the United States and entrench one-party rule.
But the walls are beginning to crumble. America is waking up. We are finding our way back to election integrity. We are talking about it fearlessly. We are connecting with others who share our convictions. We are showing up to vote, to work at our polling places, and to learn our election processes so that we have many eyes on Election Day activities. Our best strategy is to stay engaged to help stop the corruption and rebuild local elections to ensure a fair and honest process.
It won’t be easy. It won’t happen overnight. [**But it must happen.**](https://truethevote.us8.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d9ab56570d4ecf7b7df9d4c25&id=76a17b0789&e=ad8cba7e98)
We know where the problems lie – inaccurate and bloated voter rolls, mail-in ballot abuses, Election Day dirty tricks, and pervasive lawlessness. The fraud has been institutionalized.
In 2022, True the Vote developed programs that empowered people to address the biggest election problems facing their local communities.
* **Independent Voter Verification and Validation** ([I-V3.us](https://truethevote.us8.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d9ab56570d4ecf7b7df9d4c25&id=3f6bf49081&e=ad8cba7e98)) - Provides citizens with the tools to legally challenge inaccurate voter registrations. In 2022, over 2,200 volunteers successfully submitted challenges for 800,000+ ineligible registrations.

* **ProtectAmerica.Vote** \- Based on input and citizens’ nominations, True the Vote connected with approximately 900 sheriffs nationwide, providing them with peer-reviewed best practices on working with local election officials and the public to ensure a lawful election.

* **Election Integrity Tipline** \- Our online reporting platform and phone bank supported 24/7 by operators trained to support citizens who call in for help or to report election irregularities. The True the Vote team refers high risk calls to local authorities and thousands are investigated internally. In 2022, our tip line received over 10,000 reports of suspicious activities. More than 30,000 independent ballot dropbox monitors used the tip line to report findings from their observations during the 2022 midterm election cycle.

* **Ground Fusion Center-** An ongoing initiative to collect key election data and coordinate with researchers and analysts to develop Election Intelligence that can be used in litigation, legislative support, multimedia productions, and to further the efforts of law enforcement.

Additionally, we provided presentations and testimonies regarding nationwide abuse of ballot dropboxes, premiered the movie ‘2000 Mules’, hosted weekly podcasts on Locals, and fought all year long in major courtroom battles.
The pressure never let up - and we never backed down.
[**Looking ahead to 2023, we will build on our portfolio of successful programs and have plans to add even more.**](https://truethevote.us8.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d9ab56570d4ecf7b7df9d4c25&id=b879d8e2e6&e=ad8cba7e98)
As taxpayers and voters, we should be able to count on our elected officials, intelligence agencies, and law enforcement to protect our elections from foreign and domestic subversion. Until that day comes, it falls upon all of us - We the People - to defend our Republic. Together, we will build upon our wins in 2022 and continue to fight for fair and honest elections.
Please help us continue to stand strong - in court, in media, with law enforcement and legislatures, online, and in community. The battle to secure the 2024 election cycle begins now. We need your support more than ever before. [**If you would, please consider making a year-end donation to help fund our work.**](https://truethevote.us8.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d9ab56570d4ecf7b7df9d4c25&id=a35ac6ee4a&e=ad8cba7e98)**** We cannot fail. The stakes are too high.
Ever onward -
---
PS All contributions to True the Vote made by 11:59 pm December 31st, 2022 will count as a tax-deduction for the 2022 tax year.
|
---
|
---
| [PLEASE HELP SUPPORT TRUE THE VOTE](https://truethevote.us8.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d9ab56570d4ecf7b7df9d4c25&id=0654b01d67&e=ad8cba7e98)
---
|
---
| | | | |
---
_Copyright © 2022 True the Vote, All rights reserved._
You are receiving this email because you opted in for updates via the True the Vote website.

**Our mailing address is:**
True the VotePO Box 3109# 19128Houston, TX 77253-3109
[Add us to your address book](https://truethevote.us8.list-manage.com/vcard?u=d9ab56570d4ecf7b7df9d4c25&id=10c8087c36)

Want to change how you receive these emails?
You can [update your preferences](https://truethevote.us8.list-manage.com/profile?u=d9ab56570d4ecf7b7df9d4c25&id=10c8087c36&e=ad8cba7e98&c=86705c1d6b) or [unsubscribe from this list](https://truethevote.us8.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=d9ab56570d4ecf7b7df9d4c25&id=10c8087c36&e=ad8cba7e98&c=86705c1d6b).

|

body:
| **BREAKING: The GOP launches brutal attacks on the January 6th Committee after the Committee suggested criminals charges for Trump**

**[**We're sending a letter to the January 6th Committee thanking them for their brave work and we need your signature! We only have 460 more spots open for top Democrats to sign – sign right now! »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/37745?t=6&akid=5934%2E878179%2Epfv0I8)** | [DONATE $5 RIGHT NOW AND SIGN THE THANK YOU LETTER »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/37745?t=7&akid=5934%2E878179%2Epfv0I8)
---
**To: The January 6th Committee
From: [dpwillis67@gmail.com](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/37745?t=8&akid=5934%2E878179%2Epfv0I8)**

Dear January 6th Committee,

Exposing Donald Trump’s involvement in the January 6th attempted insurrection is saving our Democracy.

I know that persevering through brutal attacks from Trump’s MAGA stooges in Congress wasn’t easy.

But, spending countless hours combing over evidence to find the truth about that horrific day was crucial work.

Last week, you made me proud to be an American.

I’m so proud that we have leaders like YOU who are working to give the American people the truth about the attack on our Democracy.

**The bravery you’ve shown in standing up for what’s right will go down in history.**

Despite the attacks you’ve endured and the censorship you’ve received, you’ve never wavered.

**So thank you. Our country is better because of your leadership.** [**Today, I’m signing this letter and making a donation to VPP to keep electing leaders like you all.**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/37745?t=9&akid=5934%2E878179%2Epfv0I8) | [DONATE $5 TO SIGN THE LETTER »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/37745?t=10&akid=5934%2E878179%2Epfv0I8)
---
Sincerely,

A Grateful Democrat
| | Paid for by VPP
(www.protectvoting.org)
Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. 401 2nd Ave. S, Ste. 303, Seattle, WA 98104

The Voter Protection Project is on an important mission: To end partisan gerrymandering and voter suppression by electing candidates at the state and federal levels that are forceful advocates for voting rights. Our enfranchisement agenda includes working to ensure that every state has automatic voter registration and online voter registration, convenient early voting, and independent redistricting commissions to ensure politicians are not able to draw their own maps.

Please, consider making a [one-time contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/121?t=1&akid=5934%2E878179%2Epfv0I8). **Or, to make the most powerful lasting impact and help us oust Republicans up and down the ballot who attack our right to vote, make a[monthly sustaining contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/122?t=2&akid=5934%2E878179%2Epfv0I8).**

[Click here](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/fewer-emails/?t=3&akid=5934%2E878179%2Epfv0I8) to request fewer emails. Click here to [unsubscribe](https://act.protectvoting.org/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=11&akid=5934%2E878179%2Epfv0I8). | | [Please: Pitch In Once A Month To Protect Voting Rights »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/123?t=4&akid=5934%2E878179%2Epfv0I8)
---
[Donate Just One Time »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/124?t=5&akid=5934%2E878179%2Epfv0I8)

---

The only way there’s any chance of making up the $14,927 we still need before 2022 is over is if everyone who receives this email donates AT LEAST $5. | Peter, JD really needs your help. If JD doesn’t have the support he needs to hit his final goal then 2023 is not going to be good for Conservatives. body:
[ stdin# ][IMG]

Peter Willis, after
providing the critical margin of victory through our organizing for
Warnock and Fetterman, we're hitting the ground running to make sure a
progressive defeat Kyrsten Sinema in Arizona.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/8172?source=OR_EM_FR_25SP_20221230_2_6&t=1011&akid=3841%2E5885482%2EAsLzhy ]Our work is critical for influencing the direction the Senate goes next
year and keeping big-money puppets like Sinema out of power. Can we count
on you to send a donation before our end-of-year deadline as a founding
donor of our Defeat Sinema Fund?

— — — Begin Forwarded Message — — —

Peter Willis,

Since our founding, Our Revolution has lived
on the frontlines of the fight to deliver results for the working class by
organizing to elect progressives and advance popular policies.

In 2022, we delivered the critical margins of victory for Senator Warnock,
Senator Fetterman, and nine new progressive members of Congress!

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/8172?source=OR_EM_FR_25SP_20221230_2_6&t=1012&akid=3841%2E5885482%2EAsLzhy ]Now, we are leading the fight to defeat Kyrsten Sinema in 2024 because
early investments are critical to outmaneuvering corporate power. Can we
count on you to contribute before our end-of-year deadline as a founding
donor of our Defeat Sinema Fund?

If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation to
Our Revolution will process immediately.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/8173?refcode=OR_EM_FR_25SP_20221230_2_6&t=1013&akid=3841%2E5885482%2EAsLzhy ]CHIP IN $10 NOW » [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/8174?refcode=OR_EM_FR_25SP_20221230_2_6&t=1014&akid=3841%2E5885482%2EAsLzhy ]CHIP IN $18 NOW »
[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/8175?refcode=OR_EM_FR_25SP_20221230_2_6&t=1015&akid=3841%2E5885482%2EAsLzhy ]CHIP IN $27 NOW » [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/8176?refcode=OR_EM_FR_25SP_20221230_2_6&t=1016&akid=3841%2E5885482%2EAsLzhy ]CHIP IN $100 NOW »
[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/8177?refcode=OR_EM_FR_25SP_20221230_2_6&t=1017&akid=3841%2E5885482%2EAsLzhy ]CHIP IN $250 NOW » [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/8172?refcode=OR_EM_FR_25SP_20221230_2_6&t=1018&akid=3841%2E5885482%2EAsLzhy ]CHIP IN ANOTHER AMOUNT »

Our big wins in Georgia and PA mean Senate committees will be in
Democratic control — giving Bernie Sanders the ability to investigate
corporate greed in his new role as Chair of the HELP Committee, and the
establishment is striking back.

Big money floods the pockets of shills like Sinema and authoritarian
Republicans who will subvert democracy for personal gain, but we have each
other. Our movement is growing, there is important work to be done in the
year to come, and we need to hit the ground running.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/8172?source=OR_EM_FR_25SP_20221230_2_6&t=1019&akid=3841%2E5885482%2EAsLzhy ]Please help fund our organizing in 2023 by pitching in before our
end-of-year deadline!

In solidarity,

Our Revolution

name: Tom Fitton
subject: Big Election Integrity Victory
body: Happy New Year! [INSIDE JW] New York City Removes 441,083 Ineligible Names from Voter Rolls Thanks to Judicial Watch! [https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=61609&L=28641&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cueW91dHViZS5jb20vd2F0Y2g/dj1qWXFkanNTeGItRSZ1dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycyZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1DTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0Q=] We just settled [https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=61609&L=28638&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2p3LXYtdmFsZW50aW5lLXNldHRsZW1lbnQtMDM5NTIvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJzJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUNNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRA==] a federal election integrity lawsuit against New York City after the city removed 441,083 ineligible names from the voter rolls and promised to take reasonable steps to clean its voter registration lists in the future. We filed the lawsuit [https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=61609&L=27650&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvbnljLXZvdGVyLXJvbGxzLz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycyZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1DTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0Q=] in July after the city failed to clean voter rolls for years. The lawsuit, filed under the National Voter Registration Act [https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=61609&L=435&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVzdGljZS5nb3YvY3J0L25hdGlvbmFsLXZvdGVyLXJlZ2lzdHJhdGlvbi1hY3QtMTk5My1udnJhP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJzJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUNNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRA==] (NVRA), pointed out that New York City removed only 22 names under federal law over six years (_Judicial Watch v Valentine et al._ [https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=61609&L=27639&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL255LWJvYXJkLW9mLWVsZWN0aW9ucy1udnJhLWp1bHktMjAyMi1jb21wbGFpbnQtMDM5NTIvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJzJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUNNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRA==] (No.1:22-cv-03952)). Our suit detailed how New York City’s “own recent data concedes that there were only 22 total” removals under this provision “during a six-year period, in a city of over 5.5 million voters. These are ludicrously small numbers of removals given the sizable populations of these counties.” Moreover, the “almost complete failure of Kings, Queens, New York, Bronx, and Richmond Counties, over a period of at least six years, to remove voters” under a key provision of federal law “means that there are untold numbers of New York City registrations for voters who are ineligible to vote at their listed address because they have changed residence or are otherwise ineligible to vote.” The settlement details how the city responded to our notice about its voting roll deficiencies with a massive clean-up: > [The Board of Elections] notified Judicial Watch that, in February > 2022, they removed, pursuant to Section 8(d)(1)(B) of the NVRA, > 82,802 registrations in Bronx County, 128,093 in Kings County, > 145,891 in New York County, 66,010 in Queens County, and 18,287 in > Richmond County, for a total of 441,083 registrations. > > [The Board of Elections] notified Judicial Watch that going forward > they intend to cancel registrations pursuant to Section 8(d)(1)(B) > in each odd-numbered year in the months following a federal > election. Specifically, the city also agrees to track in detail and report its voter roll maintenance efforts through 2025: > For both 2023 and 2025 ... the [Board of Elections] will notify > Judicial Watch ... on or before March 31, by means of separate excel > spreadsheets for Bronx County, Kings County, New York County, Queens > County, and Richmond County, of the number of removals, including > removals pursuant to ... the NVRA, made during the previous two > years. The NVRA requires states to “conduct a general program that makes a reasonable effort to remove” from the rolls “the names of ineligible voters” who have died or changed residence. Among other things, the law requires registrations to be cancelled when voters fail to respond to address confirmation notices and then fail to vote in the next two general federal elections. In 2018, the Supreme Court confirmed that such removals are mandatory (_Husted v. A. Philip Randolph Inst_ [https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=61609&L=26310&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuc3VwcmVtZWNvdXJ0Lmdvdi9vcGluaW9ucy8xN3BkZi8xNi05ODBfZjJxMy5wZGY/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnMmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9Q01qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNE]. (138 S. Ct. 1833, 1841-42 (2018)). This historic settlement is a major victory for New York voters who will benefit from cleaner voter rolls and more honest elections. We are pleased that New York City officials quickly acted to remove 441,000 outdated registrations from the rolls. We look forward to working together under this federal lawsuit settlement to ensure New York City maintains cleaner rolls for future elections. We are a national leader in voting integrity and voting rights. As part of our work, we assembled a team of highly experienced voting rights attorneys who stopped discriminatory elections in Hawaii, and cleaned up voter rolls in California, Ohio, Indiana, and Kentucky, among other achievements [https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=61609&L=21208&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvdGFnL252cmEvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJzJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUNNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRA==]. California settled [https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=61609&L=15011&F=T&] an NVRA lawsuit with us and began the process of removing up to 1.6 million inactive names from Los Angeles County’s voter rolls. Kentucky [https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=61609&L=28639&F=T& began a cleanup of hundreds of thousands of old registrations last year after it entered into a consent decree to end another Judicial Watch lawsuit. In February 2022, we settled [https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=61609&L=26811&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvbm9ydGgtY2Fyb2xpbmEtdm90ZXItcm9sbHMtbGF3c3VpdC8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnMmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9Q01qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNE] a voter roll clean-up lawsuit against North Carolina and two of its counties after North Carolina removed over 430,000 ineligible names from the voter rolls. In March 2022, a Maryland court ruled in favor [https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=61609&L=27012&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvanVkaWNpYWwtd2F0Y2gtdmljdG9yeS1jb3VydC1ydWxlcy1hZ2FpbnN0LWdlcnJ5bWFuZGVyLWluLW1hcnlsYW5kLz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycyZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1DTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0Q=] of our challenge to Maryland’s Democratic legislature’s “extreme” congressional redistricting gerrymander. In May 2022, we sued [https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=61609&L=27407&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvbGF3c3VpdC1hZ2FpbnN0LWlsbGlub2lzLXRvLXN0b3AtY291bnRpbmctb2YtYmFsbG90cy1hZnRlci1lbGVjdGlvbi1kYXkvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJzJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUNNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRA==] Illinois on behalf of Congressman Mike Bost and two other registered Illinois voters to prevent state election officials from extending Election Day for 14 days beyond the date established by federal law. Robert Popper, Judicial Watch senior attorney, leads our election law program. Popper was previously in the Voting Section of the Civil Rights Division of the Justice Department, where he managed voting rights investigations, litigations, consent decrees, and settlements in dozens of states. HAPPY NEW YEAR! As we welcome the new year, we recall a few of our successes in 2022: > We made it more likely that voters in North Carolina > [https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=61609&L=26811&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvbm9ydGgtY2Fyb2xpbmEtdm90ZXItcm9sbHMtbGF3c3VpdC8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnMmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9Q01qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNE] > will have cleaner elections, and we are making headway for voters in > Illinois [https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=61609&L=28354&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvaWxsaW5vaXMtdm90ZS1jb3VudGluZy8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnMmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9Q01qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNE] by > challenging its election law permitting mail-in ballots to be > received as long as two weeks after Election Day. > We scored a win > [https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=61609&L=28613&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvanVkaWNpYWwtd2F0Y2gtdmljdG9yaWVzLTIvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJzJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUNNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRA==] against > two unconstitutional California quota mandates for sex, race, > ethnicity, and LGBT status requirements for corporate boards. > > We continue to uncover > [https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=61609&L=28574&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvbXJuYS10ZXN0LXJhdHMtYm9ybi13aXRoLXNrZWxldGFsLWRlZm9ybWF0aW9ucy8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnMmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9Q01qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNE] > unsettling information regarding COVID-19, including the Biden > administration’s extensive media plan > [https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=61609&L=28226&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvY292aWQtMTktdmFjY2luZS1jYW1wYWlnbi8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnMmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9Q01qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNE] for a > propaganda campaign to push the vaccine. I anticipate that Judicial Watch will carry its largest and most important caseload in our 29-year history into 2023 and engage our full arsenal of research, investigations and litigation into critically important public policy fronts. For example, uncovering critical race theory in our public institutions: We successfully settled a civil rights lawsuit [https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=61609&L=27982&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvbWEtc2Nob29sLWRpc3RyaWN0LXNldHRsZXMtbGF3c3VpdC8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnMmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9Q01qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNE] that we filed on behalf of a Massachusetts teacher who lost his position as head football coach after raising concerns about the promotion of critical race theory and Black Lives Matter propaganda in his daughter’s seventh-grade history class. We uncovered [https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=61609&L=27536&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvY3J0LXByb3BhZ2FuZGEtYXQtd2VzdC1wb2ludC8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnMmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9Q01qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNE] critical race theory instructional materials from the U.S. Military Academy, West Point, including a PowerPoint slide with a graphic titled “MODERN-DAY SLAVERY IN THE USA.” We are pursuing similar lawsuits against the U.S. Naval Academy [https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=61609&L=27741&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvanctc3Vlcy1mb3ItbmF2YWwtYWNhZGVteS1jcnQtcmVjb3Jkcy8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnMmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9Q01qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNE] and the U.S. Air Force Academy [https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=61609&L=28598&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvanctc3Vlcy1haXItZm9yY2UtYWNhZGVteS8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnMmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9Q01qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNE] to see if they are teaching similar racist, anti-American propaganda. We’re certainly eager to learn more about Hunter Biden [https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=61609&L=28275&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvaHVudGVyLWJpZGVuLWludmVzdGlnYXRpb24vP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJzJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUNNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRA==] and apparent efforts by the administration to block investigations. After the Allied victory over Hitler in 1945, but before the victory over Japan, Winston Churchill found himself in a similar situation of looking both back and forward. He declared [https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=61609&L=28642&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYmJjLmNvLnVrL25ld3Nyb3VuZC80ODIwMTc0OV06P3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJzJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUNNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRA== “We may allow ourselves a brief period of rejoicing, but let us not forget for a moment the toil and efforts that lie ahead.” Dark clouds hang over our country, but they only motivate us. As Churchill also observed, “This is no time for ease and comfort. It is time to dare and endure.” We are grateful that you are with us as we enter 2023. All of us wish you and yours a Happy New Year! I hope you’ll make a special New Year’s contribution in support of our essential work ahead. Until next week ... clean_body: Happy New Year! [INSIDE JW] New York City Removes 441,083 Ineligible Names from Voter Rolls Thanks to Judicial Watch! [=] We just settled [==] a federal election integrity lawsuit against New York City after the city removed 441,083 ineligible names from the voter rolls and promised to take reasonable steps to clean its voter registration lists in the future. We filed the lawsuit [=] in July after the city failed to clean voter rolls for years. The lawsuit, filed under the National Voter Registration Act [==] (NVRA), pointed out that New York City removed only 22 names under federal law over six years (Judicial Watch v Valentine et al. [==] (No.1:22-cv-03952)). Our suit detailed how New York City’s “own recent data concedes that there were only 22 total” removals under this provision “during a six-year period, in a city of over 5.5 million voters. These are ludicrously small numbers of removals given the sizable populations of these counties.” Moreover, the “almost complete failure of Kings, Queens, New York, Bronx, and Richmond Counties, over a period of at least six years, to remove voters” under a key provision of federal law “means that there are untold numbers of New York City registrations for voters who are ineligible to vote at their listed address because they have changed residence or are otherwise ineligible to vote.” The settlement details how the city responded to our notice about its voting roll deficiencies with a massive clean-up: [The Board of Elections] notified Judicial Watch that, in February 2022, they removed, pursuant to Section 8(d)(1)(B) of the NVRA, 82,802 registrations in Bronx County, 128,093 in Kings County, 145,891 in New York County, 66,010 in Queens County, and 18,287 in Richmond County, for a total of 441,083 registrations. [The Board of Elections] notified Judicial Watch that going forward they intend to cancel registrations pursuant to Section 8(d)(1)(B) in each odd-numbered year in the months following a federal election. Specifically, the city also agrees to track in detail and report its voter roll maintenance efforts through 2025: For both 2023 and 2025 ... the [Board of Elections] will notify Judicial Watch ... on or before March 31, by means of separate excel spreadsheets for Bronx County, Kings County, New York County, Queens County, and Richmond County, of the number of removals, including removals pursuant to ... the NVRA, made during the previous two years. The NVRA requires states to “conduct a general program that makes a reasonable effort to remove” from the rolls “the names of ineligible voters” who have died or changed residence. Among other things, the law requires registrations to be cancelled when voters fail to respond to address confirmation notices and then fail to vote in the next two general federal elections. In 2018, the Supreme Court confirmed that such removals are mandatory (Husted v. A. Philip Randolph Inst []. (138 S. Ct. 1833, 1841-42 (2018)). This historic settlement is a major victory for New York voters who will benefit from cleaner voter rolls and more honest elections. We are pleased that New York City officials quickly acted to remove 441,000 outdated registrations from the rolls. We look forward to working together under this federal lawsuit settlement to ensure New York City maintains cleaner rolls for future elections. We are a national leader in voting integrity and voting rights. As part of our work, we assembled a team of highly experienced voting rights attorneys who stopped discriminatory elections in Hawaii, and cleaned up voter rolls in California, Ohio, Indiana, and Kentucky, among other achievements [==]. California settled [] an NVRA lawsuit with us and began the process of removing up to 1.6 million inactive names from Los Angeles County’s voter rolls. Kentucky [== began a cleanup of hundreds of thousands of old registrations last year after it entered into a consent decree to end another Judicial Watch lawsuit. In February 2022, we settled [] a voter roll clean-up lawsuit against North Carolina and two of its counties after North Carolina removed over 430,000 ineligible names from the voter rolls. In March 2022, a Maryland court ruled in favor [=] of our challenge to Maryland’s Democratic legislature’s “extreme” congressional redistricting gerrymander. In May 2022, we sued [==] Illinois on behalf of Congressman Mike Bost and two other registered Illinois voters to prevent state election officials from extending Election Day for 14 days beyond the date established by federal law. Robert Popper, Judicial Watch senior attorney, leads our election law program. Popper was previously in the Voting Section of the Civil Rights Division of the Justice Department, where he managed voting rights investigations, litigations, consent decrees, and settlements in dozens of states. HAPPY NEW YEAR! As we welcome the new year, we recall a few of our successes in 2022: We made it more likely that voters in North Carolina [] will have cleaner elections, and we are making headway for voters in Illinois [] by challenging its election law permitting mail-in ballots to be received as long as two weeks after Election Day. We scored a win [==] against two unconstitutional California quota mandates for sex, race, ethnicity, and LGBT status requirements for corporate boards. We continue to uncover [] unsettling information regarding COVID-19, including the Biden administration’s extensive media plan [] for a propaganda campaign to push the vaccine. I anticipate that Judicial Watch will carry its largest and most important caseload in our 29-year history into 2023 and engage our full arsenal of research, investigations and litigation into critically important public policy fronts. For example, uncovering critical race theory in our public institutions: We successfully settled a civil rights lawsuit [] that we filed on behalf of a Massachusetts teacher who lost his position as head football coach after raising concerns about the promotion of critical race theory and Black Lives Matter propaganda in his daughter’s seventh-grade history class. We uncovered [] critical race theory instructional materials from the U.S. Military Academy, West Point, including a PowerPoint slide with a graphic titled “MODERN-DAY SLAVERY IN THE USA.” We are pursuing similar lawsuits against the U.S. Naval Academy [] and the U.S. Air Force Academy [] to see if they are teaching similar racist, anti-American propaganda. We’re certainly eager to learn more about Hunter Biden [==] and apparent efforts by the administration to block investigations. After the Allied victory over Hitler in 1945, but before the victory over Japan, Winston Churchill found himself in a similar situation of looking both back and forward. He declared [== “We may allow ourselves a brief period of rejoicing, but let us not forget for a moment the toil and efforts that lie ahead.” Dark clouds hang over our country, but they only motivate us. As Churchill also observed, “This is no time for ease and comfort. It is time to dare and endure.” We are grateful that you are with us as we enter 2023. All of us wish you and yours a Happy New Year! I hope you’ll make a special New Year’s contribution in support of our essential work ahead. Until next week ... body:
Team, as we come to the end of 2022 and welcome 2023, in the coming days we will see a new Congress sworn in, and will see a new Republican majority arrive in the House...| | | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/56651702/385617456/1060786640?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NDk5NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzZjNmZGRhMS05NDg4LWVkMTEtOWQ3YS0wMDIyNDgzMmU2MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=8PhCsgX0zD0hcv20TPUhEgnzui6tQZjDS9Ca6Y3tCps=)
---
| | **Friend, as we come to the end of 2022 and welcome 2023, in the coming days we will see a new Congress sworn in, and will see a new Republican majority arrive in the House.
** **What will they focus on? Previous experience offers some possibilities...**
---
* **Weaponizing the debt ceiling to try and force cuts to Social Security and Medicare.**
* **Pushing for tax cuts for billionaires and big business.**
* **Pursuing nonsensical investigations to try and score cheap political points.**

---
[STAND WITH US](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/56651703/385617457/1060786640?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NDk5NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzZjNmZGRhMS05NDg4LWVkMTEtOWQ3YS0wMDIyNDgzMmU2MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=8PhCsgX0zD0hcv20TPUhEgnzui6tQZjDS9Ca6Y3tCps=)
---
| | **This is who the modern Republican party is. Focused on theater rather than substance, and working hardest for big business and the top 1%, not for regular Americans.
** **THIS is why we need to make their time in the majority as short as possible. Brendan is working with his colleagues to send the incoming Republican majority packing come November 2024, and we need your help to make that happen. ** **Your support is what allows Brendan and his fellow Democrats to compete in districts all across the country, and that will never be more true than as we approach the 2024 election and work to restore a Democratic majority in the House.
** **So as we close out 2022, we hope you'll continue to stand with us as we take this fight into the new year.**
---
[STAND WITH US](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/56651704/385617458/1060786640?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NDk5NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzZjNmZGRhMS05NDg4LWVkMTEtOWQ3YS0wMDIyNDgzMmU2MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=8PhCsgX0zD0hcv20TPUhEgnzui6tQZjDS9Ca6Y3tCps=)
---
**Thanks for your continued support.
** **Team Boyle**

---

name: Mia Bonta
subject: Time is Running Out - Register TONIGHT!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Mia Bonta for Assembly 2022, FPPC# 1437973
body:
Hi Peter,
I’ll keep this short – time is running out to register for a mail-in ballot [https://ademelections.com/register/mail] for the upcoming CA Democratic Party ADEM Delegate Election. Registration closes tomorrow, December 31st at noon.
>>> DON’T MISS YOUR CHANCE, REGISTER TO VOTE BEFORE IT’S TOO LATE <<< [https://ademelections.com/register/mail]
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP30/1/89949/images/adem.png]
The Holidays and New Year are not the only thing around the corner – there’s also an important local election only a month away! In January, the CA Democratic Party will hold the biennial Assembly District Delegate election. Delegates play a crucial role in steering party priorities, endorsing candidates, and serving as vital links between our community and the Democratic Party.
I’m proud to support 14 local leaders running to represent AD-18 on the 2022 ADEM Progressive & Labor Unity Slate!
Unity Slate members are champions for progressive values, working people, and effective advocacy. Each candidate on this slate is deeply rooted to our local community and are forces for change; advancing important issues such as housing affordability, healthcare, and access to public education.
I’m a proud supporter of the Labor & Progressive Unity Slate, a group of 14 leaders from AD-18 who are champions for progressive values, working people, and effective advocacy. This slate is deeply rooted in our local community and together, they are a force for change.
The Progressive & Labor Unity Slate is endorsed by:
Assemblymember Mia Bonta Peter
State Controller, Malia Cohen
Oakland Mayor-Elect Sheng Thao
Oakland Council President Nikki Fortunato Bas
Oakland Councilmember Dan Kalb
Emeryville Mayor John Bauters
Emeryville Councilmember Ally Medina
Emeryville Councilmember David Mourra
SEIU-UHW (healthcare workers)
Alameda County Building and Construction Trades Council
Black Women Organized for Political Action
The way the Democratic Party approaches issues such as housing affordability, healthcare, and criminal justice reform will be decided by the elected delegates. This is an opportunity to show our AD-18 values at all levels, beginning within our very own party.
Peter, every vote counts in this election – join me and get your ballot now! [https://ademelections.com/register/mail]
Ballots will be mailed out on January 6th. Save the PIN which is emailed to you when you register online. When voting, please vote for all 14 Progressive & Labor Unity Slate members in the order they appear on your ballot: Zac Bowling, Mayra Alvarado, Carlotta Brown, Victor Flores, Doug Bloch, Courtney Welch, Fay Eastman, Jada Taylor Folse, Renia Webb, Ivan Garcia, Rowena Brown, Alex Randolph, Seth Steward, and Kevin Jenkins.
Please help spread the word and forward this email to five people close to you! It’s the only way we will ensure we have a Democratic Party that represents our values and interests.
In community,
Assemblymember Mia Bonta
Paid for by Mia Bonta for Assembly 2022, FPPC# 1437973
1241 High St, Oakland, CA 94601 | 415-891-9751 | volunteer@miabonta.com
https://www.facebook.com/miabonta [https://www.facebook.com/miabonta] https://twitter.com/MiaBonta [https://twitter.com/MiaBonta] https://instagram.com/mia.bonta [https://instagram.com/mia.bonta] https://www.linkedin.com/in/mialisabonta/ [https://www.linkedin.com/in/mialisabonta/]
If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please unsubscribe: https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/YS7WRSJS4UGbNPwJK1c7zA2?unsubscribedata=5s95CLohaFf4OnB%2FAJv0FVJ8GZQk2naTsv72zqy2EOQ7YR8jfO9iSaBK6gip%2FzFSYEoc6%2BqbWRF%2FzCMsNQ9F%2BYkjt99xHb5As9i0MgOUAOXJxI4pg3wkM5hyoOTagZsvFBhfQalKm3Gb%2F5V29GPUU3GzWQdPq7dv63mYW5iZID4EupIMoHW0fOhXRCFSP8nuPTAPWNJryTqafyfntQn9rUkWImKTwHjkGXax0AKP40gXW9s6CDq%2F51z5GxsGpxG9Ktu0B4bhdLVshxTk8FL1R%2BZnAaq5GfAV93RwCXVaF4wXhPQHztJS3MNn4A6WFJRrEh10BVvcqghRXUEz4%2BGWGig2hAjYXufGjkY3RGRFDFCXGH%2Bg6UFwS41id55ZsJ33iRe%2FahUvjr8iYghgmIi8v6j%2BASLz5dVfTivHxv3Ui0113NdeGF9X6o59XyDxq%2F35 .

name: Lizzie Pannill Fletcher
subject: Looking Back on 2022, and Looking Ahead
body:
ELIZABETH PANNILL FLETCHER FOR CONGRESS [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP10/1/82676/images/lizzie-reelect-logo-outlined.png]
Tomorrow night, we ring in the New Year. Today, I am thinking about all we achieved together this eventful year.
Building on our successful work together in 2018 and 2020, we won the 2022 election decisively in the newly configured TX-07. We were out and about all over our new district, meeting new people and hearing about the issues on their minds and the things they want to see happen in Washington.
Because of the work we have done together, I was able to talk to people about the critical work we have done for our community and our country this year. Here are just some of the highlights:
JANUARY
Congress started the year by taking action on one of the most pressing issues facing our democracy: voter suppression. I voted for, and the House passed, the John R. Lewis Act to ensure every American can exercise their right to vote without obstruction.
FEBRUARY
Texans rely on the Postal Service to deliver critical mail — from paychecks to medications — on time. I helped pass, and President Biden signed, a measure securing the USPS’ financial future and making mail service more reliable for everyone who depends on it.
MARCH
#TeamLizzie capped off our work to get people to the polls in the Texas primary — talking with people across the newly drawn district where 70% of voters were new!
APRIL
I voted to send urgently needed resources and aid to Ukraine to demonstrate that our country stands with Ukraine as it defends its sovereignty in the face of Russia’s unprovoked and unconscionable invasion.
MAY
Texas and other states have dropped qualified providers like Planned Parenthood from their Medicaid programs, leaving over 8,000 Texans without their trusted source of reproductive health care. I led a group of 100 Members of Congress calling on the Administration to reinstate Planned Parenthood as a Medicaid provider to ensure Texans can access the care they need.
JUNE
In response to more devastating mass shootings in Uvalde and across the country, I helped pass the largest gun safety legislation in nearly 30 years, banning large-capacity magazines, closing the “boyfriend” loophole, and encouraging safe storage of firearms.
JULY
After the Supreme Court’s decision to overturn Roe v. Wade , I introduced, and the House passed, a bill to protect Americans’ constitutional right to travel between states for the critical reproductive health care they need, a right that Texas legislators and people across the country have actively threatened.
AUGUST
Congress passed and President Biden signed into law the Inflation Reduction Act and the CHIPS and Science Act — both of which I worked on in my committees — to allow Medicare to negotiate lower drug prices, implement smart energy policy while reducing greenhouse gas emissions, strengthen domestic supply chains, and secure our country’s scientific leadership.
SEPTEMBER
A bill I introduced to give disabled veterans the opportunity to update outdated adaptations to their vehicles for their medical needs if their disabilities prevent them from driving passed the House, and later the Senate, and is awaiting the President's signature.
OCTOBER
Our official team hit a milestone of successfully closing 9,000 cases for residents of TX-07 since I was sworn in in 2019, helping them navigate processes and cases before federal agencies, connecting seniors with Social Security and Medicare benefits and veterans with health care and resources from the VA.
NOVEMBER
Ahead of Election Day, #TeamLizzie knocked on thousands of doors, sent nearly 7,000 postcards and four mailers, made calls and sent texts, and delivered yard signs. We ran ads that reached nearly 2.5 million Texas households. All of our hard work paid off on election night when we brought home our third win in TX-07!
DECEMBER
Earlier this month, President Biden signed the Respect for Marriage Act — a bill I co-sponsored and helped pass the House — to ensure equal treatment under the law for same-sex and interracial couples.
This year has been a team effort, and I could not be more grateful for what you've done to make it possible to do this important work.
2023 will bring new challenges. I’ll head back to Washington next week for the beginning of the 118th Congress, and all it will bring. I remain committed to working together when we can and to standing up and fighting back for our communities and our country when we must.
We will also continue our important campaign work in 2023. We know a thing or two about that here on #TeamLizzie, as our work helped Democrats regain the House majority in 2018. We are already working to make sure we do so again in 2024, and that we help elect Democrats up and down the ballot in this important Presidential election year.
You have an important role to play in that work. One way you can help us get off to a great start right now is by contributing before tomorrow’s end-of-year FEC deadline. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/folf221230?refcode=folf221230]
Thanks for considering this request, and thanks for all you do. I look forward to working with you in 2023, and I wish you all the best for a very happy new year.
Together, we can do anything,
Lizzie Sign-off [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP10/1/82676/images/lizzie_signature-1.png]
DONATE [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/folf-footer]
PAID FOR BY FRIENDS OF LIZZIE FLETCHER, A JOINT FUNDRAISING COMMITTEE AUTHORIZED BY ELIZABETH PANNILL FLETCHER FOR CONGRESS AND LEADING PEOPLE FORWARD PAC.
Don't want to receive these emails anymore? unsubscribe: https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/yiT4vmtXrUCBvy-D5k60rg2?unsubscribedata=RDoX5L%2FuC7k%2FU70huEfrSSYw4MP9Z3pvgC4quwGc1DOEvhRRkZU9uoX0SNZlDl586OKTxoSdVZBihg7KorKXr0NrOAnY7K%2B9hgRRGE%2BJG%2B6Y3lSbRGtmWRukJRTvgGaDB0xFFFnbfMzeHdpjD%2Bt3lcCd7nqHJDdoLLxYzpw%2Bv%2B%2BO0sW5cXtlQW64dgJrY%2BcQHmhBf42rVHnOBhzWheb3CB7cAuwaiPK9WAxcisCB5J6e1rVLINDCQ6jDRXVujU%2BaCT1UwdJsx%2Bu6NxdeiWkYDGuCDHHlR3R5TC9W2adVoRp0gNmJx9oL5VBBhMGOgX62qzKrTZJjQqTLjsJLtnG3VySGZlrug9mxh1I8r%2FahgId9yFRJ5tgij5GH9UoPw2KVMiVqxeuV0B443vmVIqi3aSYGMfmCaAx4trizhhaWBPrCrxlKV13d5H4UML0dgQnc www.lizziefletcher.com [https://lizziefletcher.com/]

I led a group of 100 Members of Congress calling on the Administration to reinstate Planned Parenthood as a Medicaid provider to ensure Texans can access the care they need. JUNE In response to more devastating mass shootings in Uvalde and across the country, I helped pass the largest gun safety legislation in nearly 30 years, banning large-capacity magazines, closing the “boyfriend” loophole, and encouraging safe storage of firearms. JULY After the Supreme Court’s decision to overturn Roe v. Wade , I introduced, and the House passed, a bill to protect Americans’ constitutional right to travel between states for the critical reproductive health care they need, a right that Texas legislators and people across the country have actively threatened. AUGUST Congress passed and President Biden signed into law the Inflation Reduction Act and the CHIPS and Science Act — both of which I worked on in my committees — to allow Medicare to negotiate lower drug prices, implement smart energy policy while reducing greenhouse gas emissions, strengthen domestic supply chains, and secure our country’s scientific leadership. SEPTEMBER A bill I introduced to give disabled veterans the opportunity to update outdated adaptations to their vehicles for their medical needs if their disabilities prevent them from driving passed the House, and later the Senate, and is awaiting the President's signature. OCTOBER Our official team hit a milestone of successfully closing 9,000 cases for residents of TX-07 since I was sworn in in 2019, helping them navigate processes and cases before federal agencies, connecting seniors with Social Security and Medicare benefits and veterans with health care and resources from the VA. NOVEMBER Ahead of Election Day, #TeamLizzie knocked on thousands of doors, sent nearly 7,000 postcards and four mailers, made calls and sent texts, and delivered yard signs. We ran ads that reached nearly 2.5 million Texas households. All of our hard work paid off on election night when we brought home our third win in TX-07! DECEMBER Earlier this month, President Biden signed the Respect for Marriage Act — a bill I co-sponsored and helped pass the House — to ensure equal treatment under the law for same-sex and interracial couples. This year has been a team effort, and I could not be more grateful for what you've done to make it possible to do this important work. 2023 will bring new challenges. I’ll head back to Washington next week for the beginning of the 118th Congress, and all it will bring. I remain committed to working together when we can and to standing up and fighting back for our communities and our country when we must. We will also continue our important campaign work in 2023. We know a thing or two about that here on #TeamLizzie, as our work helped Democrats regain the House majority in 2018. We are already working to make sure we do so again in 2024, and that we help elect Democrats up and down the ballot in this important Presidential election year. You have an important role to play in that work. One way you can help us get off to a great start right now is by contributing before tomorrow’s end-of-year FEC deadline. [] Thanks for considering this request, and thanks for all you do. I look forward to working with you in 2023, and I wish you all the best for a very happy new year. Together, we can do anything, Lizzie Sign-off [] DONATE [] PAID FOR BY FRIENDS OF LIZZIE FLETCHER, A JOINT FUNDRAISING COMMITTEE AUTHORIZED BY ELIZABETH PANNILL FLETCHER FOR CONGRESS AND LEADING PEOPLE FORWARD PAC. Don't want to receive these emails anymore? unsubscribe: www.lizziefletcher.com []
